_meta.source: OpenScriptures/strongs (CC-BY-SA), derived from James Strong, 1894 (public domain)
_meta.license: CC-BY-SA (OpenScriptures JSON), underlying work public domain (James Strong, 1890/1894)

H1.def: a primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application.

H3.def: from the same as H24 (אָבִיב); a green plant.

H4.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3 (אֵב); a green plant.

H5.def: of foreign origin; Abagtha, a eunuch of Xerxes.

H6.gloss: to wander away, i.e. lose oneself
H6.def: a primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. lose oneself; by implication to perish (causative, destroy).
H6.kjv: break, destroy(-uction), not escape, fail, lose, (cause to, make) perish, spend, and surely, take, be undone, utterly, be void of, have no way to flee

H7.gloss: to wander away, i.e. lose oneself
H7.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H6 (אָבַד); properly, to wander away, i.e. lose oneself; by implication to perish (causative, destroy).

H8.def: active of participle of H6 (אָבַד); (concrete) wretched or (abstract) destruction.

H9.def: from H6 (אָבַד); Compare H10 (אֲבַדֹּה).; concrete, something lost; abstract, destruction, i.e. Hades.

H10.def: the same as H9 (אֲבֵדָה), miswritten for H11 (אֲבַדּוֹן); a perishing.

H11.def: intensive from H6 (אָבַד); abstract, a perishing; concrete, Hades.

H14.gloss: to breathe after, i.e. (figuratively) to be acquiescent
H14.def: a primitive root; to breathe after, i.e. (figuratively) to be acquiescent.
H14.kjv: consent, rest content will, be willing

H16.def: from H14 (אָבָה) (in the sense of bending toward); the papyrus.

H17.def: from H14 (אָבָה) (in the sense of desiring); want.

H18.def: from H75 (אָבַס); a manger or stall.

H19.def: from an unused root (apparently meaning to turn); brandishing of a sword.

H20.def: of uncertain derivation; a melon (only plural).

H21.def: from H1 (אָב); fatherly; Abi, Hezekiah's mother.

H22.gloss: Abiel, the name of two Israelites
H22.def: from H1 (אָב) and H410 (אֵל); father (i.e. possessor) of God; Abiel, the name of two Israelites.

H23.def: from H1 (אָב) and H622 (אָסַף); father of gathering (i.e. gatherer); Abiasaph, an Israelite.

H24.gloss: green, i.e. a young ear of grain
H24.def: from an unused root (meaning to be tender); green, i.e. a young ear of grain; hence, the name of the month Abib or Nisan.
H24.kjv: Abib, ear, green ears of corn (not maize)

H25.def: from H1 (אָב) and H1391 (גִּבְעוֹן); father (i.e. founder) of Gibon; Abi-Gibon, perhaps an Israelite.

H26.gloss: Abigail or Abigal, the name of two Israelitesses
H26.def: or shorter אֲבִיגַל; from H1 (אָב) and H1524 (גִּיל); father (i.e. source) of joy; Abigail or Abigal, the name of two Israelitesses.

H27.def: from H1 (אָב) and H1777 (דִּין); father of judgment (i.e. judge); Abidan, an Israelite.

H28.gloss: Abida, a son of Abraham by Keturah
H28.def: from H1 (אָב) and H3045 (יָדַע); father of knowledge (i.e. knowing); Abida, a son of Abraham by Keturah.

H29.def: or prolonged אֲבִיָּהוּ ; from H1 (אָב) and H3050 (יָהּ); father (i.e. worshipper) of Jah; Abijah, the name of several Israelite men and two Israelitesses.

H30.def: from H1 (אָב) and H1931 (הוּא); father (i.e. worshipper) of Him (i.e. God); Abihu, a son of Aaron.

H31.gloss: Abihud, the name of two Israelites
H31.def: from H1 (אָב) and H1935 (הוֹד); father (i.e. possessor) of renown; Abihud, the name of two Israelites.

H32.def: or (more correctly) אֲבִיחַיִל ; from H1 (אָב) and H2428 (חַיִל); father (i.e. possessor) of might; Abihail or Abichail, the name of three Israelites and two Israelitesses.

H33.gloss: an Abiezrite or descendant of Abiezer
H33.def: from H44 (אֲבִיעֶזֶר) with the article inserted; father of the Ezrite; an Abiezrite or descendant of Abiezer.

H34.def: from H14 (אָבָה), in the sense of want (especially in feeling); destitute.

H35.def: from H14 (אָבָה); provocative of desire; the caper berry (from its stimulative taste).

H36.def: from H1 (אָב) and H2898 (טוּב); father of goodness (i.e. good); Abitub, an Israelite.

H37.gloss: Abital, a wife of King David
H37.def: from H1 (אָב) and H2919 (טַל); father of dew (i.e. fresh); Abital, a wife of King David.

H38.gloss: Abijam (or Abijah), a king of Judah
H38.def: from H1 (אָב) and H3220 (יָם); father of (the) sea (i.e. seaman); Abijam (or Abijah), a king of Judah.

H39.def: from H1 (אָב) and an elsewhere unused (probably foreign) word; father of Mael (apparently some Arab tribe); Abimael, a son of Joktan.

H40.def: from H1 (אָב) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); father of (the) king; Abimelek, the name of two Philistine kings and of two Israelites.

H41.gloss: Abinadab, the name of four Israelites
H41.def: from H1 (אָב) and H5068 (נָדַב); father of generosity (i.e. liberal); Abinadab, the name of four Israelites.

H42.def: from H1 (אָב) and H5278 (נֹעַם); father of pleasantness (i.e. gracious); Abinoam, an Israelite.

H43.def: contracted from H23 (אֲבִיאָסָף); Ebjasaph, an Israelite.

H44.gloss: Abiezer, the name of two Israelites
H44.def: from H1 (אָב) and H5829 (עֵזֶר); father of help (i.e. helpful); Abiezer, the name of two Israelites.

H45.def: from H1 (אָב) and an unused root of uncertain derivation; probably, father of strength (i.e. valiant); Abialbon, an Israelite.

H47.kjv: angel, bull, chiefest, mighty (one), stout(-hearted), strong (one), valiant

H48.gloss: Abiram, the name of two Israelites
H48.def: from H1 (אָב) and H7311 (רוּם); father of height (i.e. lofty); Abiram, the name of two Israelites.

H49.def: from H1 (אָב) and H7686 (שָׁגָה); father of error (i.e. blundering); Abishag, a concubine of David.

H50.gloss: Abishua, the name of two Israelites
H50.def: from H1 (אָב) and H7771 (שׁוֹעַ); father of plenty (i.e. prosperous); Abishua, the name of two Israelites.

H51.def: from H1 (אָב) and H7791 (שׁוּר); father of (the) wall (i.e. perhaps mason); Abishur, an Israelite.

H52.def: or (shorter) אַבְשַׁי ; from H1 (אָב) and H7862 (שַׁי); father of a gift (i.e. probably generous); Abishai, an Israelite.

H53.def: or (shortened) אַבְשָׁלוֹם ; from H1 (אָב) and H7965 (שָׁלוֹם); father of peace (i.e. friendly); Abshalom, a son of David; also (the fuller form) a later Israelite.

H54.def: contracted from H1 (אָב) and H3498 (יָתַר); father of abundance (i.e. liberal); Ebjathar, an Israelite.

H55.def: a primitive root; probably to coil upward.

H58.def: from an unused root (meaning to be grassy); a meadow.
H58.kjv: plain. Compare also the proper names beginning with Abel-

H59.gloss: Abel, the name of two places in Palestine
H59.def: from H58 (אָבֵל); a meadow; Abel, the name of two places in Palestine..

H61.def: apparently from H56 (אָבַל) through the idea of negation; nay, i.e. truly or yet.

H62.gloss: Abel of Beth-maakah, a place in Palestine
H62.def: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4601 (מַעֲכָה); meadow of Beth-Maakah; Abel of Beth-maakah, a place in Palestine.

H63.gloss: Abel hash-Shittim, a place in Palestine
H63.def: from H58 (אָבֵל) and the plural of H7848 (שִׁטָּה), with the article inserted; meadow of the acacias; Abel hash-Shittim, a place in Palestine.

H64.def: from H58 (אָבֵל) and the plural of H3754 (כֶּרֶם); meadow of vineyards; Abel-Keramim, a place in Palestine.

H65.def: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H4246 (מְחֹלָה)xlit mᵉchôwlâh corrected to mᵉchôlâh; meadow of dancing; Abel-Mecholah, a place in Palestine.

H66.def: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H4325 (מַיִם); meadow of water; Abel-Majim, a place in Palestine.

H67.def: from H58 (אָבֵל) and H4714 (מִצְרַיִם); meadow of Egypt; Abel-Mitsrajim, a place in Palestine.

H68.def: from the root of H1129 (בָּנָה) through the meaning to build; a stone.
H68.kjv: carbuncle, mason, plummet, (chalk-, hail-, head-, sling-) stone(-ny), (divers) weight(-s)

H70.def: from the same as H68 (אֶבֶן); a pair of stones (only dual); a potter's wheel or a midwife's stool (consisting alike of two horizontal disks with a support between).

H71.def: perhaps feminine of H68 (אֶבֶן); stony;  Compare H549 (אֲמָנָה).; Abanah, a river near Damascus.

H72.def: from H68 (אֶבֶן) and H5828 (עֵזֶר) with the article inserted; stone of the help; Eben-ha-Ezer, a place in Palestine.

H74.def: or (fully) אֲבִינֵר ; from H1 (אָב) and H5216 (נִיר); father of light (i.e. enlightening); Abner, an Israelite.

H76.def: (by reduplication) from an unused root (meaning to belch forth); an inflammatory pustule (as eruption).

H77.def: from an unused root probably meaning to gleam; conspicuous; Ebets, a place in Palestine.

H78.def: from the same as H76 (אֲבַעְבֻּעָה); splendid; Ibtsan, an Israelite.

H79.def: a primitive root, probably to float away (as vapor), but used only as denominative from H80 (אָבָק); to bedust, i.e. grapple.

H80.def: from root of H79 (אָבַק); light particles (as volatile).

H81.def: feminine of H80 (אָבָק); light particles (as volatile).

H85.gloss: Abraham, the later name of Abram
H85.def: contracted from H1 (אָב) and an unused root (probably meaning to be populous); father of a multitude; Abraham, the later name of Abram.

H86.def: probably an Egyptian word meaning; kneel.

H87.gloss: Abram, the original name of Abraham
H87.def: contracted from H48 (אֲבִירָם); high father; Abram, the original name of Abraham.

H88.gloss: Oboth, a place in the Desert
H88.def: plural of H178 (אוֹב); water-skins; Oboth, a place in the Desert.

H89.def: of uncertain derivation (compare H90 (אֲגַג)); Age, an Israelite.

H90.gloss: Agag, a title of Amalekitish kings
H90.def: or אֲגָג; of uncertain derivation (compare H89 (אָגֵא)); flame; Agag, a title of Amalekitish kings.

H91.gloss: an Agagite or descendent (subject) of Agag
H91.def: patrial or patronymic from H90 (אֲגַג); an Agagite or descendent (subject) of Agag.

H92.gloss: a band, bundle, knot, or arch
H92.def: feminine passive participle of an unused root (meaning to bind); a band, bundle, knot, or arch.

H93.def: prob of Persian origin; a nut.

H94.gloss: Agur, a fanciful name for Solomon
H94.def: passive participle of H103 (אָגַר); gathered (i.e. received among the sages); Agur, a fanciful name for Solomon.

H95.gloss: something gathered, i.e. perhaps a grain or berry
H95.def: from the same as H94 (אָגוּר); properly, something gathered, i.e. perhaps a grain or berry; used only of a small (silver) coin.

H96.def: from an unused root (meaning to flow down or together as drops); a reservoir.

H97.def: dual of H96 (אֶגֶל).; a double pond; Eglajim, a place in Moab.

H98.def: from an unused root (meaning to collect as water); a marsh; hence a rush (as growing in swamps); hence a stockade of reeds.

H99.def: probably from the same as H98 (אֲגַם) (in the sense of stagnant water); figuratively, sad.

H100.def: from the same as H98 (אֲגַם); a marshy pool (others from a different root, a kettle); by implication; a bulrush (as growing there); collectively a rope of bulrushes.

H101.def: probably from H5059 (נָגַן); a bowl (as pounded out hollow).

H102.gloss: (only plural) wings of an army, or crowds of troops
H102.def: probably from H5062 (נָגַף) (through the idea of impending); a cover or heap; i.e.; (only plural) wings of an army, or crowds of troops.

H104.gloss: an epistle (as carried by a state courier or postman)
H104.def: (Aramaic) of Persian origin; an epistle (as carried by a state courier or postman).

H106.def: from H1640 (גָּרַף) (in the sense of grasping); the clenched hand.

H108.def: from the same as H181 (אוּד) (in the sense of enveloping); a fog.

H110.def: probably from H109 (אָדַב) (in the sense of chastisement) and H410 (אֵל); disciplined of God; Adbeel, a son of Ishmael.

H111.def: probably an orthographical variation for H2301 (חֲדַד); Adad (or Hadad), an Edomite.

H112.def: of uncertain derivation; Iddo, an Israelite.

H113.def: or (shortened) אָדֹן; from an unused root (meaning to rule); sovereign, i.e. controller (human or divine).
H113.kjv: lord, master, owner. Compare also names beginning with 'Adoni-'

H114.def: probably intensive for H113 (אָדוֹן); powerful; Addon, apparently an Israelite.

H115.def: dual from H142 (אָדַר) (in the sense of eminence); double mound; Adorajim, a place in Palestine.

H117.def: from H142 (אָדַר); wide or (generally) large; figuratively, powerful.
H117.kjv: excellent, famous, gallant, glorious, goodly, lordly, mighty(-ier one), noble, principal, worthy

H118.def: of Persian derivation; Adalja, a son of Haman.

H119.def: to show blood (in the face), i.e.; flush or turn rosy.

H120.def: ruddy, i.e., a human being (an individual or the species, mankind, etc.).

H121.gloss: Adam the name of the first man, also of a place in Palestine
H121.def: the same as H120 (אָדָם); Adam the name of the first man, also of a place in Palestine.

H123.gloss: Edom, the elder twin-brother of Jacob
H123.def: or (fully) אֱדוֹם ; from H122 (אָדֹם); red (see Genesis 25:25); Edom, the elder twin-brother of Jacob; hence the region (Idumaea) occupied by him.

H124.gloss: redness, i.e. the ruby, garnet, or some other red gem
H124.def: from H119 (אָדַם); redness, i.e. the ruby, garnet, or some other red gem.

H126.gloss: Admah, a place near the Dead Sea
H126.def: contracted for H127 (אֲדָמָה); earthy; Admah, a place near the Dead Sea.

H127.def: from H119 (אָדַם); soil (from its general redness).

H128.def: the same as H127 (אֲדָמָה); Adamah, a place in Palestine.

H129.def: from H127 (אֲדָמָה); earthy; Adami, a place in Palestine.

H130.gloss: an Edomite, or descendants from (or inhabitants of) Edom
H130.def: or (fully) אֱדוֹמִי ; patronymic from H123 (אֱדֹם); See H726 (אֲרוֹמִי).; an Edomite, or descendants from (or inhabitants of) Edom.

H131.def: plural of H121 (אָדָם); red spots; Adummim, a pass in Palestine.

H132.gloss: reddish (of the hair or the complexion)
H132.def: or (fully) אַדְמוֹנִי ; from H119 (אָדַם); reddish (of the hair or the complexion).

H133.def: probably of Persian derivation; Admatha, a Persian nobleman.

H134.gloss: a basis (of a building, a column, etc.)
H134.def: from the same as H113 (אָדוֹן) (in the sense of strength); a basis (of a building, a column, etc.).

H135.def: intensive from the same as H134 (אֶדֶן); firm; Addan, an Israelite.

H136.gloss: the Lord (used as a proper name of God only)
H136.def: an emphatic form of H113 (אָדוֹן); the Lord (used as a proper name of God only).

H137.def: from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H966 (בֶּזֶק); lord of Bezek; Adoni-Bezek; a Canaanitish king.

H138.gloss: Adonijah, the name of three Israelites
H138.def: original (prolonged) אֲדֹנִיָּהוּ ; from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H3050 (יָהּ); lord (i.e. worshipper) of Jah; Adonijah, the name of three Israelites.

H139.def: from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H6664 (צֶדֶק); lord of justice; Adoni-Tsedek, a Canaanitish king.

H140.gloss: Adonikam, the name of one or two Israelites
H140.def: from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H6965 (קוּם); lord of rising (i.e. high); Adonikam, the name of one or two Israelites.

H141.def: from H113 (אָדוֹן) and H7311 (רוּם); lord of height; Adoniram, an Israelite.

H142.gloss: to expand, i.e. be great or (figuratively) magnificent
H142.def: a primitive root; to expand, i.e. be great or (figuratively) magnificent.

H143.def: probably of foreign derivation; perhaps meaning fire; Adar, the 12th Hebrew month.

H144.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H143 (אֲדָר); Adar, the 12th Hebrew month.

H145.def: from H142 (אָדַר); amplitude, i.e. (concrete) a mantle; also (figuratively) splendor.

H146.def: intensive from H142 (אָדַר); ample; Addar, a place in Palestine; also an Israelite.

H147.def: (Aramaic) intensive, from a root corresponding to H142 (אָדַר); ample, i.e. a threshing-floor.

H148.def: (Aramaic) from the same as H147 (אִדַּר), and H1505 (גְּזַר); a chief diviner, or astrologer.

H149.def: (Aramaic) probably of Persian origin; quickly or carefully.

H150.def: of Persian origin; a daric or Persian coin.

H151.def: contracted for H141 (אֲדֹנִירָם); Adoram (or Adoniram), an Israelite.

H152.def: from H142 (אָדַר) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); splendor of (the) king; Adrammelek, the name of an Assyrian idol, also of a son of Sennacherib.

H153.def: (Aramaic) an orthographical variation for H1872 (דְּרַע); an arm, i.e. (figuratively) power.

H154.gloss: Edrei, the name of two places in Palestine
H154.def: from the equivalent of H153 (אֶדְרָע); mighty; Edrei, the name of two places in Palestine.

H155.gloss: something ample (as a large vine, a wide dress)
H155.def: feminine of H117 (אַדִּיר); ; also the same as H145 (אֶדֶר); something ample (as a large vine, a wide dress).

H156.def: a primitive root; to tread out (grain).

H157.def: a primitive root; to have affection for (sexually or otherwise).

H158.gloss: affection (in a good or a bad sense)
H158.def: from H157 (אָהַב); affection (in a good or a bad sense).

H159.gloss: affection (in a good or a bad sense)
H159.def: from H156 (אָדַשׁ); meaning the same as H158 (אַהַב); affection (in a good or a bad sense).

H160.gloss: affection (in a good or a bad sense)
H160.def: feminine of H158 (אַהַב) and meaning the same; affection (in a good or a bad sense).

H161.def: from an unused root meaning to be united; unity; Ohad, an Israelite.

H162.def: apparently a primitive word expressing pain exclamatorily; Oh!.

H163.def: probably of foreign origin; Ahava, a river of Babylonia.

H164.gloss: Ehud, the name of two or three Israelites
H164.def: from the same as H161 (אֹהַד); united; Ehud, the name of two or three Israelites.

H165.def: apparently an orthographical variation for H346 (אַיֵּה); where.
H165.kjv: I will be (Hosea 13:10,14) (which is often the rendering of the same Hebrew form from H1961 (הָיָה))

H166.def: a primitive root; to be clear.

H167.def: a denominative from H168 (אֹהֶל); to tent.

H168.gloss: a tent (as clearly conspicuous from a distance)
H168.def: from H166 (אָהַל); a tent (as clearly conspicuous from a distance).

H169.def: the same as H168 (אֹהֶל); Ohel, an Israelite.

H170.gloss: Oholah, a symbolic name for Samaria
H170.def: in form a feminine of H168 (אֹהֶל), but in fact for אׇהֳלָהּ; from H168 (אֹהֶל); her tent (i.e. idolatrous sanctuary); Oholah, a symbolic name for Samaria.

H171.def: from H168 (אֹהֶל) and H1 (אָב); tent of (his) father; Oholiab, an Israelite.

H172.gloss: Oholibah, a symbolic name for Judah
H172.def: (similarly with H170 (אׇהֳלָה)) for אׇהֳלִיבָהּ ; from H168 (אֹהֶל); my tent (is) in her; Oholibah, a symbolic name for Judah.

H173.def: from H168 (אֹהֶל) and H1116 (בָּמָה); tent of (the) height; Oholibamah, a wife of Esau.

H174.def: or (feminine) אֲהָלוֹת (only used thus in the plural); of foreign origin; aloe wood (i.e. sticks).

H175.def: of uncertain derivation; Aharon, the brother of Moses.

H176.def: presumed to be the 'constructive' or genitival form of אַו ; short for H185 (אַוָּה); desire (and so probably in Proverbs 31:4); hence (by way of alternative) or, also if.
H176.kjv: also, and, either, if, at the least, nor, or, otherwise, then, whether

H177.def: from H176 (אוֹ) and H410 (אֵל); wish of God; Uel, and Israelite.

H178.gloss: a mumble, i.e. a water skin (from its hollow sound)
H178.def: from the same as H1 (אָב) (apparently through the idea of prattling a father's name); properly, a mumble, i.e. a water skin (from its hollow sound); hence a necromancer (ventriloquist, as from a jar).

H179.def: probably from H56 (אָבַל); mournful; Obil, an Ishmaelite.

H180.def: or (shortened) אֻבָל ; from H2986 (יָבַל) (in the sense of H2988 (יָבָל)); a stream.

H181.gloss: a poker (for turning or gathering embers)
H181.def: from an unused root meaning to rake together; a poker (for turning or gathering embers).

H182.def: or (shortened) אֹדוֹת (only thus in the plural); from the same as H181 (אוּד); turnings (i.e. occasions); (adverb) on account of.

H183.def: a primitive root; to wish for.
H183.kjv: covet, (greatly) desire, be desirous, long, lust (after)

H184.def: a primitive root; to extend or mark out.

H186.def: perhaps by permutation for H5813 (עֻזִּי), strong; Uzai, an Israelite.

H187.def: of uncertain derivation; Uzal, a son of Joktan.

H188.def: probably from H183 (אָוָה) (in the sense of crying out after); lamentation; also interjectionally Oh!.

H189.def: probably from H183 (אָוָה); desirous; Evi, a Midianitish chief.

H190.def: feminine of H188 (אוֹי); lamentation; also interjectionally Oh!.

H191.def: from an unused root (meaning to be perverse); (figuratively) silly.

H192.def: of Aramaic derivation and probably meaning soldier of Merodak; Evil-Merodak, a Babylonian king.

H193.def: from an unused root meaning to twist, i.e. (by implication) be strong; the body (as being rolled together); also powerful.

H194.def: or (shortened) אֻלַי; from H176 (אוֹ); if not; hence perhaps.
H194.kjv: if so be, may be, peradventure, unless

H195.gloss: the Ulai (or Eulaeus), a river of Persia
H195.def: of Persian derivation; the Ulai (or Eulaeus), a river of Persia.

H196.def: from H191 (אֱוִיל); silly, foolish; hence (morally) impious.

H197.gloss: a vestibule (as bound to the building)
H197.def: or (shortened), אֻלָם ; from H481 (אָלַם) (in the sense of tying); a vestibule (as bound to the building).

H198.gloss: Ulam, the name of two Israelites
H198.def: appar, from H481 (אָלַם) (in the sense of dumbness); solitary; Ulam, the name of two Israelites.

H199.def: apparently a variation of H194 (אוּלַי); however or on the contrary.
H199.kjv: as for, but, howbeit, in very deed, surely, truly, wherefore

H200.def: from the same as H191 (אֱוִיל); silliness.

H201.def: from H559 (אָמַר); talkative; Omar, a grandson of Esau.

H202.def: probably from the same as H205 (אָוֶן) (in the sense of effort, but successful); ability, power, (figuratively) wealth.

H203.def: the same as H202 (אוֹן); On, an Israelite.

H204.def: or (shortened); אֹן; of Egyptian derivation; On, a city of Egypt.

H205.def: from an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant (hence, to exert oneself, usually in vain; to come to naught); strictly nothingness; also trouble. vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
H205.kjv: affliction, evil, false, idol, iniquity, mischief, mourners(-ing), naught, sorrow, unjust, unrighteous, vain, vanity, wicked(-ness). Compare H369 (אַיִן)

H206.def: the same as H205 (אָוֶן); idolatry; Aven, the contemptuous synonym of three places, one in Coele-Syria, one in Egypt (On), and one in Palestine (Bethel).
H206.kjv: Aven. See also H204 (אוֹן), H1007 (בֵּית אָוֶן)

H207.def: or (shortened) אֹנוֹ; prolonged from H202 (אוֹן); strong; Ono, a place in Palestine.

H208.gloss: Onam, the name of an Edomite and of an Israelite
H208.def: a variation of H209 (אוֹנָן); strong; Onam, the name of an Edomite and of an Israelite.

H209.def: a variation of H207 (אוֹנוֹ); strong; Onan, a son of Judah.

H210.def: perhaps a corruption of H211 (אוֹפִיר); Uphaz, a famous gold region.

H211.def: or (shortened) אֹפִיר; and אוֹפִר; of uncertain derivation; Ophir, the name of a son of Joktan, and of a gold region in the East.

H212.def: or (shortened) אֹפָן; from an unused root meaning to revolve; a wheel.

H213.def: a primitive root; to press; (by implication) to be close, hurry, withdraw.

H215.def: a primitive root; to be (causative, make) luminous (literally and metaphorically).
H215.kjv: break of day, glorious, kindle, (be, en-, give, show) light (-en, -ened), set on fire, shine

H216.def: illumination or (concrete) luminary (in every sense, including lightning, happiness, etc.).

H217.def: from H215 (אוֹר); flame; hence (in the plural) the East (as being the region of light).

H218.def: the same as H217 (אוּר); Ur, a place in Chaldaea; also an Israelite.

H219.def: feminine of H216 (אוֹר); luminousness, i.e. (figuratively) prosperity; also a plant (as being bright).

H220.def: by transposition for H723 (אֻרְוָה); a stall.

H221.gloss: Uri, the name of three Israelites
H221.def: from H217 (אוּר); fiery; Uri, the name of three Israelites.

H222.gloss: Uriel, the name of two Israelites
H222.def: from H217 (אוּר) and H410 (אֵל); flame of God; Uriel, the name of two Israelites.

H223.gloss: Urijah, the name of one Hittite and five Israelites
H223.def: or (prolonged) אוּרִיָּהוּ ; from H217 (אוּר) and H3050 (יָהּ); flame of Jah; Urijah, the name of one Hittite and five Israelites.

H224.def: plural of H217 (אוּר); lights; Urim, the oracular brilliancy of the figures in the high-priest's breastplate.

H225.def: a primitive root; properly, to come, i.e. (implied) to assent.

H226.def: probably from H225 (אוּת) (in the sense of appearing); a signal (literally or figuratively), as aflag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc..

H227.def: a demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
H227.kjv: beginning, for, from, hitherto, now, of old, once, since, then, at which time, yet

H228.def: (Aramaic) or אֲזָה (Aramaic); to kindle; (by implication) to heat.

H229.def: probably from H231 (אֵזוֹב); hyssop-like; Ezbai, an Israelite.

H232.def: from H246 (אֲזִקִּים); something girt; a belt, also a band.

H233.def: probably from H227 (אָז); at that time.

H234.def: from H2142 (זָכַר); a reminder; specifically remembrance-offering.

H235.gloss: to go away, hence, to disappear
H235.def: a primitive root; to go away, hence, to disappear.
H235.kjv: fail, gad about, go to and fro (but in Ezekiel 27:19 the word is rendered by many 'from Uzal,' by others 'yarn'), be gone (spent)

H236.def: (Aramaic) the same as H235 (אָזַל); to depart.

H237.gloss: Ezel, a memorial stone in Palestine
H237.def: from H235 (אָזַל); departure; Ezel, a memorial stone in Palestine.

H238.gloss: to broaden out the ear (with the hand)
H238.def: a primitive root; probably to expand; but used only as a denominative from H241 (אֹזֶן); to broaden out the ear (with the hand), i.e. (by implication) to listen.
H238.kjv: give (perceive by the) ear, hear(-ken). See H239 (אָזַן)

H239.def: a primitive root (rather identical with H238 (אָזַן) through the idea of scales as if two ears); to weigh, i.e. (figuratively) ponder.

H240.gloss: a spade or paddle (as having a broad end)
H240.def: from H238 (אָזַן); a spade or paddle (as having a broad end).

H241.gloss: broadness. i.e. (concrete) the ear (from its form in man)
H241.def: from H238 (אָזַן); broadness. i.e. (concrete) the ear (from its form in man).
H241.kjv: advertise, audience, displease, ear, hearing, show

H242.def: from H238 (אָזַן) and H7609 (שֶׁאֱרָה); plot of Sheerah (i.e. settled by him); Uzzen-Sheerah, a place in Palestine.

H243.def: from H238 (אָזַן) and H8396 (תָּבוֹר); flats (i.e. tops) of Tabor (i.e. situated on it); Aznoth-Tabor, a place in Palestine.

H244.def: from H241 (אֹזֶן); having (quick) ears; Ozni, an Israelite; also an Oznite (collectively), his descendant.

H245.def: from H238 (אָזַן) and H3050 (יָהּ); heard by Jah; Azanjah, an Israelite.

H248.def: a variation for H2220 (זְרוֹעַ); the arm.

H249.gloss: a spontaneous growth, i.e. native (tree or persons)
H249.def: from H2224 (זָרַח) (in the sense of springing up); a spontaneous growth, i.e. native (tree or persons).
H249.kjv: bay tree, (home-) born (in the land), of the (one's own) country (nation)

H250.gloss: an Ezrachite or descendant of Zerach
H250.def: patronymic from H2246 (חֹבָב); an Ezrachite or descendant of Zerach.

H251.gloss: a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationsh
H251.def: a primitive word; a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationship and metaphorical affinity or resemblance (like father)).
H251.kjv: another, brother(-ly); kindred, like, other. Compare also the proper names beginning with 'Ah-' or 'Ahi-'

H252.gloss: a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationsh
H252.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H251 (אָח); a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationship and metaphorical affinity or resemblance).

H253.def: a variation for H162 (אֲהָהּ); Oh! (expressive of grief or surprise).

H254.def: of uncertain derivation; a fire-pot or chafing dish.

H255.gloss: a howler or lonesome wild animal
H255.def: probably from H253 (אָח); a howler or lonesome wild animal.

H256.def: once (by contraction) אֶחָב (Jeremiah 29:22); from H251 (אָח) and H1 (אָב); brother (i.e. friend) of (his) father; Achab, the name of a king of Israel and of a prophet at Babylon.

H257.def: from H251 (אָח) and H995 (בִּין); brother (i.e. possessor) of understanding; Achban, an Israelite.

H258.def: perhaps a primitive root; to unify, i.e. (figuratively) collect (one's thoughts).

H259.def: a numeral from H258 (אָחַד); properly, united, i.e. one; or (as an ordinal) first.
H259.kjv: a, alike, alone, altogether, and, any(-thing), apiece, a certain, (dai-) ly, each (one), eleven, every, few, first, highway, a man, once, one, only, other, some, together,

H260.gloss: a bulrush or any marshy grass (particularly that along th
H260.def: of uncertain (perhaps Egyptian) derivation; a bulrush or any marshy grass (particularly that along the Nile).

H261.gloss: Echud, the name of three Israelites
H261.def: from H258 (אָחַד); united; Echud, the name of three Israelites.

H262.def: from H2331 (חָוָה) (in the sense of H2324 (חֲוָא)); an utterance.

H265.def: by reduplication from H251 (אָח); brotherly; Achoach, an Israelite.

H266.gloss: an Achochite or descendant of Achoach
H266.def: patronymic from H264 (אַחֲוָה); an Achochite or descendant of Achoach.

H267.def: perhaps from H251 (אָח) and H4325 (מַיִם); brother (i.e. neighbour) of water; Achumai, an Israelite.

H268.def: or (shortened) אָחֹר ; from H299 (אֲחִירַע); the hinder part; hence (adverb) behind, backward; also (as facing north) the West.
H268.kjv: after(-ward), back (part, -side, -ward), hereafter, (be-) hind(-er part), time to come, without

H269.def: irregular feminine of H251 (אָח); a sister (used very widely (like brother), literally and figuratively).

H270.gloss: to seize (often with the accessory idea of holding in pos
H270.def: a primitive root; to seize (often with the accessory idea of holding in possession).
H270.kjv: be affrighted, bar, (catch, lay, take) hold (back), come upon, fasten, handle, portion, (get, have or take) possess(-ion)

H271.gloss: Achaz, the name of a Jewish king and of an Israelite
H271.def: from H270 (אָחַז); possessor; Achaz, the name of a Jewish king and of an Israelite.

H272.gloss: something seized, i.e. a possession (especially of land)
H272.def: feminine passive participle from H270 (אָחַז); something seized, i.e. a possession (especially of land).

H273.def: from H270 (אָחַז); seizer; Achzai, an Israelite.

H274.gloss: Achazjah, the name of a Jewish and an Israelite king
H274.def: or (prolonged) אֲחַזְיָהוּ ; from H270 (אָחַז) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has seized; Achazjah, the name of a Jewish and an Israelite king.

H275.def: from H270 (אָחַז); seizure; Achuzzam, an Israelite.

H276.def: a variation of H272 (אֲחֻזָּה); possession; Achuzzath, a Philistine.

H277.gloss: Achi, the name of two Israelites
H277.def: from H251 (אָח); brotherly; Achi, the name of two Israelites.

H278.def: probably the same as H277 (אֲחִי); Echi, an Israelite.

H279.def: from H251 (אָח) and H517 (אֵם); brother of the mother (i.e. uncle); Achiam, an Israelite.

H281.gloss: Achijah, the name of nine Israelites
H281.def: or (prolonged) אֲחִיָּהוּ ; from H251 (אָח) and H3050 (יָהּ); brother (i.e. worshipper) of Jah; Achijah, the name of nine Israelites.

H282.def: from H251 (אָח) and H1935 (הוֹד); brother (i.e. possessor) of renown; Achihud, an Israelite.

H283.gloss: Achio, the name of three Israelites
H283.def: prolonged from H251 (אָח); brotherly; Achio, the name of three Israelites.

H284.def: from H251 (אָח) and H2330 (חוּד); brother of a riddle (i.e. mysterious); Achichud, an Israelite.

H285.gloss: Achitub, the name of several priests
H285.def: from H251 (אָח) and H2898 (טוּב); brother of goodness; Achitub, the name of several priests.

H286.def: from H251 (אָח) and H3205 (יָלַד); brother of one born; Achilud, an Israelite.

H287.def: from H251 (אָח) and H4191 (מוּת); brother of death; Achimoth, an Israelite.

H288.gloss: Achimelek, the name of an Israelite and of a Hittite
H288.def: from H251 (אָח) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); brother of (the) king; Achimelek, the name of an Israelite and of a Hittite.

H289.gloss: Achiman, the name of an Anakite and of an Israelite
H289.def: or אֲחִימָן ; from H251 (אָח) and H4480 (מִן); brother of a portion (i.e. gift); Achiman, the name of an Anakite and of an Israelite.

H290.gloss: Achimaats, the name of three Israelites
H290.def: from H251 (אָח) and the equivalent of H4619 (מַעַץ); brother of anger; Achimaats, the name of three Israelites.

H291.def: from H251 (אָח); brotherly; Achjan, an Israelite.

H292.def: from H251 (אָח) and H5068 (נָדַב); brother of liberality; Achinadab, an Israelite.

H293.gloss: Achinoam, the name of two Israelitesses
H293.def: from H251 (אָח) and H5278 (נֹעַם); brother of pleasantness; Achinoam, the name of two Israelitesses.

H294.def: from H251 (אָח) and H5564 (סָמַךְ); brother of support; Achisamak, an Israelite.

H295.gloss: Achiezer, the name of two Israelites
H295.def: from H251 (אָח) and H5828 (עֵזֶר); brother of help; Achiezer, the name of two Israelites.

H296.def: from H251 (אָח) and H6965 (קוּם); brother of rising (i.e. high); Achikam, an Israelite.

H297.def: from H251 (אָח) and H7311 (רוּם); brother of height (i.e. high); Achiram, an Israelite.

H298.gloss: an Achiramite or descendant (collectively) of Achiram
H298.def: patronymic from H297 (אֲחִירָם); an Achiramite or descendant (collectively) of Achiram.

H299.def: from H251 (אָח) and H7451 (רַע); brother of wrong; Achira, an Israelite.

H300.def: from H251 (אָח) and H7837 (שַׁחַר); brother of (the) dawn; Achishachar, an Israelite.

H301.def: from H251 (אָח) and H7891 (שִׁיר); brother of (the) singer; Achishar, an Israelite.

H302.def: from H251 (אָח) and H8602 (תָּפֵל); brother of folly; Achithophel, an Israelite.

H303.def: from the same root as H2459 (חֶלֶב); fatness (i.e. fertile); Achlab, a place in Palestine.

H304.gloss: Achlai, the name of an Israelitess and of an Israelite
H304.def: the same as H305 (אַחֲלַי); wishful; Achlai, the name of an Israelitess and of an Israelite.

H305.def: or אַחֲלֵי; prob from H253 (אָח) and a variation of H3863 (לוּא); would that!.

H306.def: perhaps from H2492 (חָלַם) (and thus dream-stone); a gem, probably the amethyst.

H307.gloss: Achmetha (i.e. Ecbatana), the summer capital of Persia
H307.def: of Persian derivation; Achmetha (i.e. Ecbatana), the summer capital of Persia.

H308.def: of uncertain derivation; Achasbai, an Israelite.

H309.def: a primitive root; to loiter (i.e. be behind); by implication to procrastinate.
H309.kjv: continue, defer, delay, hinder, be late (slack), stay (there), tarry (longer)

H310.def: from H309 (אָחַר); properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after (in various senses).
H310.kjv: after (that, -ward), again, at, away from, back (from, -side), behind, beside, by, follow (after, -ing), forasmuch, from, hereafter, hinder end, out (over) live, persecute, posterity, pursuing, remnant, seeing, since, thence(-forth), when, with

H312.def: from H309 (אָחַר); properly, hinder; generally, next, other, etc..

H313.def: the same as H312 (אַחֵר); Acher, an Israelite.

H314.def: or (shortened) אַחֲרֹן; from H309 (אָחַר); hinder; generally, late or last; specifically (as facing the east) western.
H314.kjv: after (-ward), to come, following, hind(-er, -ermost, -most), last, latter, rereward, ut(ter) most

H315.def: from H310 (אַחַר) and H251 (אָח); after (his) brother; Achrach, an Israelite.

H316.def: from H310 (אַחַר) and H2426 (חֵיל); behind (the) intrenchment (i.e. safe); Acharchel, an Israelite.

H319.gloss: the last or end, hence, the future
H319.def: from H310 (אַחַר); the last or end, hence, the future; also posterity.
H319.kjv: (last, latter) end (time), hinder (utter) -most, length, posterity, remnant, residue, reward

H320.def: (Aramaic) from H311 (אַחַר); the same as H319 (אַחֲרִית); later.

H321.def: (Aramaic) from H311 (אַחַר); the same as H317 (אׇחֳרִי); other.

H323.gloss: a satrap or governorof amain province (of Persia)
H323.def: of Persian derivation; a satrap or governorof amain province (of Persia).

H324.gloss: a satrap or governorof amain province (of Persia)
H324.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H323 (אֲחַשְׁדַּרְפַּן); a satrap or governorof amain province (of Persia).

H325.def: or (shortened) אַחַשְׁרֹשׁ (Esther 10:1); of Persian origin; Achashverosh (i.e. Ahasuerus or Artaxerxes, but in this case Xerxes), the title (rather than name) of a Persian king.

H326.def: probably of Persian derivation; an achastarite (i.e. courier); the designation (rather than name) of an Israelite.

H328.gloss: (as a noun) a necromancer (from their soft incantations)
H328.def: from an unused root perhaps meaning to move softly; (as a noun) a necromancer (from their soft incantations), (as an adverb) gently.

H329.def: from an unused root probably meaning to pierce or make fast; a thorn-tree (especially the buckthorn).

H330.def: from an unused root (probably meaning to bind); properly, twisted (yarn), i.e. tapestry.

H331.def: a primitive root; to close (the lips or ears); by analology to contract (a window by bevelled jambs).

H332.def: a primitive root; to close up.

H333.gloss: Ater, the name of three Israelites
H333.def: from H332 (אָטַר); maimed; Ater, the name of three Israelites.

H334.gloss: shut up, i.e. impeded (as to the use of the right hand)
H334.def: from H332 (אָטַר); shut up, i.e. impeded (as to the use of the right hand).

H335.def: perhaps from H370 (אַיִן); where? hence how?.
H335.kjv: how, what, whence, where, whether, which (way)

H336.def: probably identical with H335 (אַי) (through the idea of a query); not.

H338.gloss: a howler (used only in the plural)
H338.def: probably identical with H337 (אִי) (through the idea of a doleful sound); a howler (used only in the plural), i.e. any solitary wild creature.

H339.def: from H183 (אָוָה); properly, a habitable spot (as desirable); dry land, a coast, an island.

H340.gloss: to hate (as one of an opposite tribe or party)
H340.def: a primitive root; to hate (as one of an opposite tribe or party); hence to be hostile.

H341.def: or (fully) אוֹיֵב; active participle of H340 (אָיַב); hating; an adversary.

H343.def: from the same as H181 (אוּד) (in the sense of bending down); oppression; by implication misfortune, ruin.

H344.def: perhaps from H337 (אִי); the screamer, i.e. a hawk.

H345.gloss: Ajah, the name of two Israelites
H345.def: the same as H344 (אַיָּה); Ajah, the name of two Israelites.

H347.gloss: Ijob, the patriarch famous for his patience
H347.def: from H340 (אָיַב); hated (i.e. persecuted); Ijob, the patriarch famous for his patience.

H348.gloss: Izebel, the wife of king Ahab
H348.def: from H336 (אִי)lemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי and H2083 (זְבֻל); Izebel, the wife of king Ahab.

H349.def: also אֵיכָה ; and אֵיכָכָה ; prolonged from H335 (אַי); how? or how!; also where.

H350.def: from H336 (אִי)lemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי and H3519 (כָּבוֹד); (there is) no glory, i.e. inglorious; Ikabod, a son of Phineas.

H351.def: probably a variation for H349 (אֵיךְ), but not as an interogative; where.

H352.def: from the same as H193 (אוּל); properly, strength; hence, anything strong; specifically a chief (politically); also a ram (from his strength); a pilaster (as a strong support); an oak or other strong tree.
H352.kjv: mighty (man), lintel, oak, post, ram, tree

H354.def: an intensive form of H352 (אַיִל) (in the sense of ram); a stag or male deer.

H355.def: feminine of H354 (אַיָּל); a doe or female deer.

H356.def: or (shortened) אֵלוֹן ; or אֵילֹן ; from H352 (אַיִל); oak-grove; Elon, the name of a place in Palestine, and also of one Hittite, two Israelites.

H357.gloss: Ajalon, the name of five places in Palestine
H357.def: from H354 (אַיָּל); deer-field; Ajalon, the name of five places in Palestine.

H358.gloss: Elon of Beth-chanan, a place in Palestine
H358.def: from H356 (אֵילוֹן), H1004 (בַּיִת), and H2603 (חָנַן); oak-grove of (the) house of favor; Elon of Beth-chanan, a place in Palestine.

H359.gloss: Eloth or Elath, a place on the Red Sea
H359.def: or אֵילַת ; from H352 (אַיִל); trees or a grove (i.e. palms); Eloth or Elath, a place on the Red Sea.

H360.def: feminine of H353 (אֱיָל); power; by implication, protection.

H361.gloss: a pillar-space (or colonnade), i.e. a pale (or portico)
H361.def: or (shortened) אֵלָם ; or (feminine) אֵלַמָּה ; probably from H352 (אַיִל); a pillar-space (or colonnade), i.e. a pale (or portico).

H362.gloss: Elim, a place in the Desert
H362.def: plural of H352 (אַיִל); palm-trees; Elim, a place in the Desert.

H364.gloss: El-Paran, a portion of the district of Paran
H364.def: from H352 (אַיִל) and H6290 (פָּארָן); oak of Paran; El-Paran, a portion of the district of Paran.

H365.def: the same as H355 (אַיָּלָה); a doe.

H366.def: from an unused root (meaning to frighten); frightful.

H367.def: or (shortened) אֵמָה; from the same as H366 (אָיֹם); fright; concrete, an idol (as a bugbear).
H367.kjv: dread, fear, horror, idol, terrible, terror

H368.gloss: Emim, an early Canaanitish (or Maobitish) tribe
H368.def: plural of H367 (אֵימָה); terrors; Emim, an early Canaanitish (or Maobitish) tribe.

H369.def: as if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
H369.kjv: else, except, fail, (father-) less, be gone, in(-curable), neither, never, no (where), none, nor, (any, thing), not, nothing, to nought, past, un(-searchable), well-nigh, without. Compare H370 (אַיִן)

H370.gloss: where? (only in connection with prepositional prefix
H370.def: probably identical with H369 (אַיִן) in the sense of query (compare H336 (אִי)lemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי); where? (only in connection with prepositional prefix, whence).

H371.def: apparently a shortened form of H369 (אַיִן); but (like H370 (אַיִן)) an interrogative; is it not?.

H372.def: from H336 (אִי)lemma אי missing vowel, corrected to אִי and H5828 (עֵזֶר); helpless; Iezer, an Israelite.

H373.gloss: an Iezrite or descendant of Iezer
H373.def: patronymic from H372 (אִיעֶזֵר); an Iezrite or descendant of Iezer.

H374.gloss: an ephah or measure for grain
H374.def: or (shortened) אֵפָה ; of Egyptian derivation; an ephah or measure for grain; hence, a measure in general.

H375.def: from H335 (אַי) and H6311 (פֹּה); what place?; also (of time) when?; or (of means) how?.

H376.gloss: a man as an individual or a male person
H376.def: contracted for H582 (אֱנוֹשׁ) (or perhaps rather from an unused root meaning to be extant); a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term (and in such cases frequently not expressed in translation).
H376.kjv: also, another, any (man), a certain, champion, consent, each, every (one), fellow, (foot-, husband-) man, (good-, great, mighty) man, he, high (degree), him (that is), husband, man(-kind), none, one, people, person, steward, what (man) soever, whoso(-ever), worthy. Compare H802 (אִשָּׁה)

H377.gloss: to be a man, i.e. act in amanly way
H377.def: denominative from H376 (אִישׁ); to be a man, i.e. act in amanly way.

H378.gloss: Ish-Bosheth, a son of King Saul
H378.def: from H376 (אִישׁ) and H1322 (בֹּשֶׁת); man of shame; Ish-Bosheth, a son of King Saul.

H379.def: from H376 (אִישׁ) and H1935 (הוֹד); man of renown; Ishod, an Israelite.

H380.gloss: the little man of the eye
H380.def: diminutive from H376 (אִישׁ); the little man of the eye; the pupil or ball; hence, the middle (of night).

H381.def: from H376 (אִישׁ) and H2428 (חַיִל); man of might; by defective transcription (2 Samuel 23:20) אִישׁ־חַיxlit ʼÎsh-Chay corrected to ʼÎysh-Chay; as if from H376 (אִישׁ) and H2416 (חַי); living man; Ishchail (or Ish-chai), an Israelite.

H382.def: from H376 (אִישׁ) and H2897 (טוֹב); man of Tob; Ish-Tob, a place in Palestine.

H383.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H3426 (יֵשׁ); properly, entity; used only as aparticle of affirmation, there is.
H383.kjv: art thou, can, do ye, have, it be, there is (are), we will not

H384.def: perhaps from H837 (אֹשֶׁר) and H410 (אֵל); God has arrived; Ithiel, the name of an Israelite, also of a symbolical person.

H385.def: from H339 (אִי) and H8558 (תָּמָר); coast of the palm-tree; Ithamar, a son of Aaron.

H386.def: or (shortened) אֵתָן ; from an unused root (meaning to continue); permanence; hence (concrete) permanent; specifically a chieftain.

H387.gloss: Ethan, the name of four Israelites
H387.def: the same as H386 (אֵיתָן); permanent; Ethan, the name of four Israelites.

H388.gloss: Ethanim, the name of a month
H388.def: plural of H386 (אֵיתָן); always with the article; the permanent brooks; Ethanim, the name of a month.

H389.def: akin to H403 (אָכֵן); a particle of affirmation, surely; hence (by limitation) only.
H389.kjv: also, in any wise, at least, but, certainly, even, howbeit, nevertheless, notwithstanding, only, save, surely, of a surety, truly, verily, wherefore, yet (but)

H390.def: from an unused root probably meaning to strengthen; a fortress; Accad, a place in Babylon.

H391.def: from H3576 (כָּזַב); falsehood; by implication treachery.

H392.gloss: Akzib, the name of two places in Palestine
H392.def: from H391 (אַכְזָב); deceitful (in the sense of a winter-torrent which fails in summer); Akzib, the name of two places in Palestine.

H393.def: from an unused root (apparently meaning to act harshly); violent; by implication deadly; also (in a good sense) brave.

H396.def: feminine from H398 (אָכַל); something eatable, i.e. food.

H397.def: of uncertain derivation; Akish, a Philistine king.

H398.def: a primitive root; to eat (literally or figuratively).
H398.kjv: at all, burn up, consume, devour(-er, up), dine, eat(-er, up), feed (with), food, freely, in...wise(-deed, plenty), (lay) meat, quite

H399.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H398 (אָכַל); to eat (literally or figuratively).

H400.kjv: eating, food, meal(-time), meat, prey, victuals

H401.def: or אֻכָּל; apparently from H398 (אָכַל); devoured; Ucal, a fancy name.

H403.def: from H3559 (כּוּן) (compare H3651 (כֵּן)); firmly; figuratively, surely; also (advers.) but.
H403.kjv: but, certainly, nevertheless, surely, truly, verily

H404.def: a primitive root; apparently meaning to curve (as with a burden); to urge.

H405.def: from H404 (אָכַף); a load; by implication, a stroke (others dignity).

H406.def: from an unused root meaning to dig; a farmer.

H407.def: from H3784 (כָּשַׁף); fascination; Acshaph, a place in Palestine.

H408.gloss: not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative)
H408.def: a negative particle (akin to H3808 (לֹא)); not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative); once (Job 24:25) as a noun, nothing.
H408.kjv: nay, neither, never, no, nor, not, nothing (worth), rather than

H409.gloss: not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative)
H409.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H408 (אַל); not (the qualified negation, used as a deprecative); once (Job 24:25) as a noun, nothing.

H410.def: strength; as adjective mighty; especially the Almighty (but used also of any deity).

H411.def: a demonstrative particle (but only in a plural sense); these or those.

H412.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H411 (אֵל); these or those.

H413.def: (but only used in the shortened constructive form אֶל ); a primitive particle; properly, denoting motion towards, but occasionally used of a quiescent position, i.e.; near, with or among; often in general, to.
H413.kjv: about, according to, after, against, among, as for, at, because(-fore, -side), both...and, by, concerning, for, from, hath, in(-to), near, (out) of, over, through, to(-ward), under, unto, upon, whether, with(-in)

H414.def: a variation of H424 (אֵלָה)lemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה; oak; Ela, an Israelite.

H415.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H430 (אֱלֹהִים) and H3478 (יִשְׂרָאֵל); the mighty god if Jisrael; El-Elohi-Jisrael, the title given to a consecrated spot by Jacob.

H416.gloss: El-Bethel, the title given to a consecrated spot by Jacob
H416.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H1008 (בֵּית־אֵל); the God of Bethel; El-Bethel, the title given to a consecrated spot by Jacob.

H417.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H1378 (גָּבִישׁ); hail (as if a great pearl).

H418.def: by transposition for H484 (אַלְמֻגִּים); sticks of algum wood.

H419.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H1730 (דּוֹד); God has loved; Eldad, an Israelite.

H420.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H3045 (יָדַע); God of knowledge; Eldaah, a son of Midian.

H421.def: a primitive root (rather identical with H422 (אָלָה) through the idea of invocation); to bewail.

H422.gloss: to adjure, i.e. (usually in a bad sense) imprecate
H422.def: a primitive root; properly, to adjure, i.e. (usually in a bad sense) imprecate.

H424.gloss: an oak or other strong tree
H424.def: feminine of H352 (אַיִל); an oak or other strong tree.

H425.def: the same as H424 (אֵלָה)lemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה; Elah, the name of an Edomite, of four Israelites, and also of a place in Palestine.

H427.gloss: an oak or other strong tree
H427.def: A variation of H424 (אֵלָה)lemma אִלָה first vowel, corrected to אֵלָה; an oak or other strong tree.

H428.def: prolonged from H411 (אֵל); these or those.
H428.kjv: an-(the) other; one sort, so, some, such, them, these (same), they, this, those, thus, which, who(-m)

H429.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H428 (אֵלֶּה); these or those.

H430.def: plural of H433; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God.

H432.def: probably from H408 (אַל); nay, i.e. (softened) if.

H433.def: rarely (shortened) אֱלֹהַּ; probably prolonged (emphatic) from H410 (אֵל); a deity or the Deity.

H435.def: probably of foreign derivation; Elul, the sixth Jewish month.

H436.gloss: an oak or other strong tree
H436.def: prolonged from H352 (אַיִל); an oak or other strong tree.

H437.gloss: an oak or other strong tree
H437.def: a variation of H436 (אֵלוֹן); an oak or other strong tree.

H438.gloss: Allon, an Israelite, also a place in Palestine
H438.def: the same as H437 (אַלּוֹן); Allon, an Israelite, also a place in Palestine.

H439.def: from H437 (אַלּוֹן) and a variation of H1068 (בְּכִית); oak of weeping; Allon-Bakuth, a monumental tree.

H440.gloss: an Elonite or descendant (collectively) of Elon
H440.def: or rather (shortened) אֵלֹנִי; patron from H438 (אַלּוֹן); an Elonite or descendant (collectively) of Elon.

H441.def: or (shortened) אַלֻּף; from H502 (אָלַף); familiar; a friend, also gentle; hence, a bullock (as being tame; applied, although masculine, to a cow); and so, a chieftain (as notable, like neat cattle).
H441.kjv: captain, duke, (chief) friend, governor, guide, ox

H442.gloss: Alush, a place in the Desert
H442.def: of uncertain derivation; Alush, a place in the Desert.

H443.gloss: Elzabad, the name of two Israelites
H443.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H2064 (זָבַד); God has bestowed; Elzabad, the name of two Israelites.

H444.def: a primitive root; to muddle, i.e. (figuratively and intransitive) to turn (morally) corrupt.

H445.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H2603 (חָנַן); God (is) gracious; Elchanan, an Israelite.

H446.gloss: Eliab, the name of six Israelites
H446.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H1 (אָב); God of (his) father; Eliab, the name of six Israelites.

H447.gloss: Eliel, the name of nine Israelites
H447.def: from H410 (אֵל) repeated; God of (his) God; Eliel, the name of nine Israelites.

H448.def: or (contraction) אֱלִיָּתָה; from H410 (אֵל) and H225 (אוּת); God of (his) consent; Eliathah, an Israelite.

H449.def: from the same as H419 (אֶלְדָּד); God of (his) love; Elidad, an Israelite.

H450.gloss: Eljada, the name of two Israelites and of an Aramaean leader
H450.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H3045 (יָדַע); God (is) knowing; Eljada, the name of two Israelites and of an Aramaean leader.

H451.gloss: the stout part, i.e. the fat tail of the Oriental sheep
H451.def: from H422 (אָלָה) (in the original sense of strength); the stout part, i.e. the fat tail of the Oriental sheep.

H452.def: or prolonged אֵלִיָּהוּ; from H410 (אֵל) and H3050 (יָהּ); God of Jehovah; Elijah, the name of the famous prophet and of two other Israelites.

H453.def: or (fully) אֱלִיהוּא; from H410 (אֵל) and H1931 (הוּא); God of him; Elihu, the name of one of Job's friends, and of three Israelites.

H454.gloss: Eljehoenai or Eljoenai, the name of seven Israelites
H454.def: or (shortened) אֶלְיוֹעֵינַי; from H413 (אֵל) and H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and H5869 (עַיִן); towards Jehovah (are) my eyes; Eljehoenai or Eljoenai, the name of seven Israelites.

H455.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H2244 (חָבָא); God will hide; Eljachba, an Israelite.

H456.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H2779 (חֹרֶף); God of autumn; Elichoreph, an Israelite.

H457.gloss: good for nothing, by anal. vain or vanity
H457.def: apparently from H408 (אַל); good for nothing, by anal. vain or vanity; specifically an idol.
H457.kjv: idol, no value, thing of nought

H458.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); God of (the) king; Elimelek, an Israelite.

H459.def: (Aramaic) or shorter אִלֵּן; prolonged from H412 (אֵל); these.

H460.gloss: Eljasaph, the name of two Israelites
H460.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H3254 (יָסַף); God (is) gatherer; Eljasaph, the name of two Israelites.

H461.gloss: Eliezer, the name of a Damascene and of ten Israelites
H461.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H5828 (עֵזֶר); God of help; Eliezer, the name of a Damascene and of ten Israelites.

H462.def: probably contracted for H454 (אֶלְיְהוֹעֵינַי); Elienai, an Israelite.

H463.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H5971 (עַם); God of (the) people; Eliam, an Israelite.

H464.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H6337 (פָּז); God of gold; Eliphaz, the name of one of Job's friends, and of a son of Esau.

H465.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H6419 (פָּלַל); God of judgment; Eliphal, an Israelite.

H466.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H6395 (פָּלָה); God of his distinction; Eliphelehu, an Israelite.

H467.gloss: Eliphelet or Elpelet, the name of six Israelites
H467.def: or (shortened) אֱלְפֶּלֶט; from H410 (אֵל) and H6405 (פַּלֵּט); God of deliverance; Eliphelet or Elpelet, the name of six Israelites.

H468.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H6697 (צוּר); God of (the) rock; Elitsur, an Israelite.

H469.def: or (shortened) אֶלְצָפָן; from H410 (אֵל) and H6845 (צָפַן); God of treasure; Elitsaphan or Eltsaphan, an Israelite.

H470.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H6958 (קוֹא); God of rejection; Elika, an Israelite.

H471.gloss: Eljakim, the name of four Israelites
H471.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H6965 (קוּם); God of raising; Eljakim, the name of four Israelites.

H472.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H7651 (שֶׁבַע) (in the sense of H7650 (שָׁבַע)); God of (the) oath; Elisheba, the wife of Aaron.

H473.def: probably of foreign derivation; Elishah, a son of Javan.

H474.gloss: Elishua, the son of King David
H474.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H7769 (שׁוּעַ); God of supplication (or of riches); Elishua, the son of King David.

H475.gloss: Eljashib, the name of six Israelites
H475.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H7725 (שׁוּב); God will restore; Eljashib, the name of six Israelites.

H476.gloss: Elishama, the name of seven Israelites
H476.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H8085 (שָׁמַע); God of hearing; Elishama, the name of seven Israelites.

H477.def: contracted for H474 (אֱלִישׁוּעַ).; Elisha, the famous prophet.

H478.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H8199 (שָׁפַט); God of judgment; Elishaphat, an Israelite.

H481.def: a primitive root; to tie fast; hence (of the mouth) to be tongue-tied.
H481.kjv: bind, be dumb, put to silence

H484.def: probably of foreign derivation (used thus only in the plural); almug (i.e. probably sandle-wood) sticks.

H485.def: or (masculine) אָלֻם; passive participle of H481 (אָלַם); something bound; a sheaf.

H486.def: probably of foreign derivation; Almodad, a son of Joktan.

H487.def: from H427 (אַלָּה) and H4428 (מֶלֶךְ); oak of (the) king; Allammelek, a place in Palestine.

H488.def: prolonged from H481 (אָלַם) in the sense of bereavement; discarded (as a divorced person).

H489.def: from H481 (אָלַם) as in H488 (אַלְמָן); bereavement.

H490.def: feminine of H488 (אַלְמָן); a widow; also a desolate place.

H491.def: feminine of H488 (אַלְמָן); concrete, a widow; abstract, widowhood.

H492.def: from H489 (אַלְמֹן) in the sense of concealment; some one (i.e. so and so, without giving the name of the person or place).

H493.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H5276 (נָעֵם); God (is his) delight; Elnaam, an Israelite.

H494.gloss: Elnathan, the name of four Israelites
H494.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H5414 (נָתַן); God (is the) giver; Elnathan, the name of four Israelites.

H495.gloss: Ellasar, an early country of Asia
H495.def: probably of foreign derivation; Ellasar, an early country of Asia.

H496.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H5749 (עוּד); God has testified; Elad, an Israelite.

H497.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H5710 (עָדָה); God has decked; Eladah, an Israelite.

H498.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H5756 (עוּז) (in the sense of H5797 (עֹז)); God (is) defensive; Eluzai, an Israelite.

H499.gloss: Elazar, the name of seven Israelites
H499.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H5826 (עָזַר); God (is) helper; Elazar, the name of seven Israelites.

H500.gloss: Elale or Elaleh, a place east of the Jordan
H500.def: or (more properly) אֶלְעָלֵה; from H410 (אֵל) and H5927 (עָלָה); God (is) going up; Elale or Elaleh, a place east of the Jordan.

H501.gloss: Elasah, the name of four Israelites
H501.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H6213 (עָשָׂה); God has made; Elasah, the name of four Israelites.

H502.gloss: hence, to learn (and causatively to teach)
H502.def: a primitive root, to associate with; hence, to learn (and causatively to teach).

H503.def: denominative from H505 (אֶלֶף); causative, to make athousandfold.

H504.def: from H502 (אָלַף); a family; also (from the sense of yoking or taming) an ox or cow.

H505.gloss: hence (the ox's head being the first letter of the alphabet
H505.def: prop, the same as H504 (אֶלֶף); hence (the ox's head being the first letter of the alphabet, and this eventually used as a numeral) a thousand.

H506.gloss: hence (the ox's head being the first letter of the alphabet
H506.def: (Aramaic) or אֶלֶף; (Aramaic), corresponding to H505 (אֶלֶף); hence (the ox's head being the first letter of the alphabet, and this eventually used as a numeral) a thousand.

H507.def: the same as H505 (אֶלֶף); Eleph, a place in Palestine.

H508.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H6466 (פָּעַל); God (is) act; Elpaal, an Israelite.

H510.def: probably from H408 (אַל) and H6965 (קוּם); a non-rising (i.e. resistlessness).

H511.gloss: Elkanah, the name of several Israelites
H511.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H7069 (קָנָה); God has obtained; Elkanah, the name of several Israelites.

H512.gloss: an Elkoshite or native of Elkosh
H512.def: patrial from a name of uncertain derivation; an Elkoshite or native of Elkosh.

H513.def: probably from H410 (אֵל) and a masculine form of H8435 (תּוֹלְדָה) (compare H8434 (תּוֹלָד)); God (is) generator; Eltolad, a place in Palestine.

H514.gloss: Eltekeh or Elteke, a place in Palestine
H514.def: or (more properly) אֶלְתְּקֵה; of uncertain derivation; Eltekeh or Elteke, a place in Palestine.

H515.def: from H410 (אֵל) and H8626 (תָּקַן); God (is) straight; Eltekon, a place in Palestine.

H516.def: from H408 (אַל) and H7843 (שָׁחַת); 'Thou must not destroy'; probably the opening words to a popular song.

H517.gloss: a mother (as the bond of the family)
H517.def: a primitive word; a mother (as the bond of the family); in a wide sense (both literally and figuratively (like father)).

H518.gloss: used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
H518.def: a primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!; interrogative, whether?; or conditional, if, although; also Oh that!, when; hence, as a negative, not.
H518.kjv: (and, can-, doubtless, if, that) (not), but, either, except, more(-over if, than), neither, nevertheless, nor, oh that, or, save (only, -ing), seeing, since, sith, surely (no more, none, not), though, of a truth, unless, verily, when, whereas, whether, while, yet

H519.def: apparently a primitive word; a maidservant or female slave.

H520.def: prolonged from H517 (אֵם); properly, a mother (i.e. unit of measure, or the fore-arm (below the elbow), i.e. a cubit; also a door-base (as a bond of the entrance).
H520.kjv: cubit, hundred (by exchange for H3967 (מֵאָה)), measure, post

H521.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H520 (אַמָּה); properly, a mother (i.e. unit of measure, or the fore-arm (below the elbow), i.e. a cubit; also a door-base (as a bond of the entrance).

H522.def: the same as H520 (אַמָּה); Ammah, a hill in Palestine.

H523.gloss: a collection, i.e. community of persons
H523.def: from the same as H517 (אֵם); a collection, i.e. community of persons.

H524.gloss: a collection, i.e. community of persons
H524.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H523 (אֻמַּה); a collection, i.e. community of persons.

H525.def: from H539 (אָמַן), probably in the sense of training; like H542 (אָמָן); skilled, i.e. an architect.

H526.gloss: Amon, the name of three Israelites
H526.def: the same as H525 (אָמוֹן); Amon, the name of three Israelites.

H527.def: a variation for H1995 (הָמוֹן); a throng of people.

H528.gloss: Amon (i.e. Ammon or Amn), a deity of Egypt
H528.def: of Egyptian derivation; used only as an adjunct of H4996 (נֹא); Amon (i.e. Ammon or Amn), a deity of Egypt.

H529.def: from H539 (אָמַן); established, i.e. (figuratively) trusty; also (abstractly) trustworthiness.

H530.def: literally firmness; figuratively security; morally fidelity.

H531.def: from H553 (אָמַץ); strong; Amots, an Israelite.

H532.def: an abbreviated form for H526 (אָמוֹן); Ami, an Israelite.

H533.def: or (shortened) אַמִּץ; from H553 (אָמַץ); strong or (abstractly) strength.

H534.gloss: a summit (of a tree or mountain
H534.def: apparently from H559 (אָמַר) (in the sense of self-exaltation); a summit (of a tree or mountain.

H535.def: a primitive root; to droop; by implication to be sick, to mourn.

H538.def: from H517 (אֵם); gathering-spot; Amam, a place in Palestine.

H539.def: a primitive root; (Isaiah 30:21; interchangeable with H541 (אָמַן), to go to the right hand); properly, to build up or support; to foster as a parent or nurse; figuratively to render (or be) firm or faithful, to trust or believe, to be permanent or quiet; morally to be true or certain;.
H539.kjv: hence, assurance, believe, bring up, establish, fail, be faithful (of long continuance, stedfast, sure, surely, trusty, verified), nurse, (-ing father), (put), trust, turn to the right

H540.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H539 (אָמַן); properly, to build up or support; to foster as a parent or nurse; figuratively to render (or be) firm or faithful, to trust or believe, to be permanent or quiet; morally to be true or certain};.

H541.gloss: to take the right hand road
H541.def: denominative from H3225 (יָמִין); to take the right hand road.
H541.kjv: turn to the right. See H539 (אָמַן)

H542.def: from H539 (אָמַן) (in the sense of training); an expert.

H543.def: from H539 (אָמַן); sure; abstract, faithfulness; adverb, truly.

H545.def: feminine of H544 (אֹמֶן) (in the specific sense of training); tutelage.

H546.def: feminine form of H544 (אֹמֶן) (in its usual sense); adverb, surely.

H547.def: feminine active participle of H544 (אֹמֶן) (in the original sense of supporting); a column.

H548.gloss: something fixed, i.e. a covenant. an allowance
H548.def: feminine of H543 (אָמֵן); something fixed, i.e. a covenant. an allowance.

H549.def: the same as H548 (אֲמָנָה); Amanah, a mountain near Damascus.

H550.gloss: Amnon (or Aminon), a son of David
H550.def: or אֲמִינוֹן; from H539 (אָמַן); faithful; Amnon (or Aminon), a son of David.

H551.kjv: indeed, no doubt, surely, (it is, of a) true(-ly, -th)

H552.def: an orthographical variation of H551 (אׇמְנָם); verily.

H553.gloss: to be alert, physically (on foot) or mentally (in courage)
H553.def: a primitive root; to be alert, physically (on foot) or mentally (in courage).
H553.kjv: confirm, be courageous (of good courage, stedfastly minded, strong, stronger), establish, fortify, harden, increase, prevail, strengthen (self), make strong (obstinate, speed)

H554.gloss: of a strong color, i.e. red (others fleet)
H554.def: probably from H553 (אָמַץ); of a strong color, i.e. red (others fleet).

H557.def: from H553 (אָמַץ); strong; Amtsi, an Israelite.

H558.gloss: Amatsjah, the name of four Israelites
H558.def: or אֲמַצְיָהוּ; from H553 (אָמַץ) and H3050 (יָהּ); strength of Jah; Amatsjah, the name of four Israelites.

H559.def: a primitive root; to say (used with great latitude).
H559.kjv: answer, appoint, avouch, bid, boast self, call, certify, challenge, charge, (at the, give) command(-ment), commune, consider, declare, demand, desire, determine, expressly, indeed, intend, name, plainly, promise, publish, report, require, say, speak (against, of), still, suppose, talk, tell, term, that is, think, use (speech), utter, verily, yet

H560.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H559 (אָמַר); to say (used with great latitude).

H561.kjv: answer, appointed unto him, saying, speech, word

H562.def: the same as H561 (אֵמֶר); something said.

H563.def: (Aramaic) perhaps from H560 (אֲמַר) (in the sense of bringing forth); a lamb.

H564.gloss: Immer, the name of five Israelites
H564.def: from H559 (אָמַר); talkative; Immer, the name of five Israelites.

H565.def: or אֶמְרָה; feminine of H561 (אֵמֶר), and meaning the same; something said.

H566.gloss: Imri, the name of two Israelites
H566.def: from H564 (אִמֵּר); Imri, the name of two Israelites.

H567.gloss: an Emorite, one of the Canaanitish tribes
H567.def: probably a patronymic from an unused name derived from H559 (אָמַר) in the sense of publicity, i.e. prominence; thus, a mountaineer; an Emorite, one of the Canaanitish tribes.

H568.gloss: Amarjah, the name of nine Israelites
H568.def: or prolonged אֲמַרְיָהוּ; from H559 (אָמַר) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has said (i.e. promised); Amarjah, the name of nine Israelites.

H569.def: of uncertain (perhaps foreign) derivation; Amraphel, a king of Shinar.

H570.def: time past, i.e.; yesterday or last night.

H571.def: contracted from H539; stability; (figuratively) certainty, truth, trustworthiness.

H572.def: from H4969 (מָתַח); properly, something expansive, i.e. a bag.

H573.def: from H571 (אֶמֶת); veracious; Amittai, an Israelite.

H574.def: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to that of H4975 (מֹתֶן); well-loined (i.e. burly) or mighty.

H575.def: or אָנָה; contracted from H370 (אַיִן); where?; hence, whither?, when?; also hither and thither.

H576.def: (Aramaic) or אֲנָה; (Aramaic), corresponding to H589 (אֲנִי)lemma אֲנַי second vowel, corrected to אֲנִי; I.

H577.def: or אָנָּה; apparent contracted from H160 (אַהֲבָה) and H4994 (נָא); oh now!.

H579.def: a primitive root (perhaps rather identical with H578 (אָנָה) through the idea of contraction in anguish); to approach; hence, to meet in various senses.
H579.kjv: befall, deliver, happen, seek a quarrel

H581.def: (Aramaic) or (feminine) אִנִּיןlemma אֵנִּין first vowel, corrected to אִנִּין; (Aramaic), corresponding to H1992 (הֵם); they.

H582.gloss: a man in general (singly or collectively)
H582.def: from H605 (אָנַשׁ); properly, a mortal (and thus differing from the more dignified H120 (אָדָם)); hence,; a man in general (singly or collectively).
H582.kjv: another, (blood-) thirsty, certain, chap(-man); divers, fellow, in the flower of their age, husband, (certain, mortal) man, people, person, servant, some (of them), stranger, those, their trade. It is often unexpressed in the English versions, especially when used in apposition with another word. Compare H376 (אִישׁ)

H583.def: the same as H582 (אֱנוֹשׁ); Enosh, a son of Seth.

H586.def: (Aramaic) or אֲנַחְנָה; (Aramaic), corresponding to H587 (אֲנַחְנוּ); we.

H588.def: probably from the same root as H5170 (נַחַר); a gorge or narrow pass; Anacharath, a place in Palestine.

H589.kjv: I, (as for) me, mine, myself, we, which, who

H590.def: probably from H579 (אָנָה) (in the sense of conveyance); -a ship or (collectively) a fleet.

H593.def: from H578 (אָנָה) and H5971 (עַם); groaning of (the) people; Aniam, an Israelite.

H594.gloss: according to most a plumb-line, and to others a hook
H594.def: probably from an unused root meaning to be narrow; according to most a plumb-line, and to others a hook.

H596.def: a primitive root; to mourn, i.e. complain.

H598.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H597 (אָנַס); figuratively, to distress.

H599.gloss: to breathe hard, i.e. be enraged
H599.def: a primitive root; to breathe hard, i.e. be enraged.

H600.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H639 (אַף) (only in the plural as a singular); the face.

H601.gloss: an unclean bird, perhaps the parrot (from its irascibility)
H601.def: from H599 (אָנַף); an unclean bird, perhaps the parrot (from its irascibility).

H604.gloss: some kind of lizard, probably the gecko (from its wail)
H604.def: the same as H603 (אֲנָקָה); some kind of lizard, probably the gecko (from its wail).

H605.gloss: to be frail, feeble, or (figuratively) melancholy
H605.def: a primitive root; to be frail, feeble, or (figuratively) melancholy.

H606.def: (Aramaic) or אֱנַשׁ; (Aramaic), corresponding to H582 (אֱנוֹשׁ); a man.

H609.gloss: Asa, the name of a king and of a Levite
H609.def: of uncertain derivation; Asa, the name of a king and of a Levite.

H610.def: from H5480 (סוּךְ); anointed, i.e. an oil-flask.

H612.gloss: a bond (especially manacles of a prisoner)
H612.def: from H631 (אָסַר); a bond (especially manacles of a prisoner).

H613.gloss: a bond (especially manacles of a prisoner)
H613.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H612 (אֵסוּר); a bond (especially manacles of a prisoner).

H614.gloss: gathered, i.e. (abstractly) a gathering in of crops
H614.def: or אָסִף; from H622 (אָסַף); gathered, i.e. (abstractly) a gathering in of crops.

H615.def: from H631 (אָסַר); bound, i.e. a captive.

H616.def: for H615 (אָסִיר); bound, i.e. a captive.

H617.gloss: Assir, the name of two Israelites
H617.def: the same as H616 (אַסִּיר); prisoner; Assir, the name of two Israelites.

H618.def: from an unused root meaning to heap together; a storehouse (only in the plural).

H619.def: of uncertain derivation; Asnah, one of the Nethinim.

H620.def: of foreign derivation; Osnappar, an Assyrian king.

H621.def: of Egyptian derivation; Asenath, the wife of Joseph.

H622.def: a primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. remove (destroy, leave behind, put up, restore, etc.).
H622.kjv: assemble, bring, consume, destroy, felch, gather (in, together, up again), generally, get (him), lose, put all together, receive, recover (another from leprosy), (be) rereward, surely, take (away, into, up), utterly, withdraw

H623.def: from H622 (אָסַף); collector; Asaph, the name of three Israelites, and of the family of the first.

H624.def: passive participle of H622 (אָסַף); collected (only in the plural), i.e. a collection of offerings).

H626.def: from H622 (אָסַף); a collection of people (only adverbial).

H627.gloss: a collection of (learned) men (only in the plural)
H627.def: feminine of H624 (אָסֻף); a collection of (learned) men (only in the plural).

H628.def: by reduplication from H624 (אָסֻף); gathered up together, i.e. a promiscuous assemblage (of people).

H630.def: of Persian derivation; Aspatha, a son of Haman.

H631.def: a primitive root; to yoke or hitch; by analogy, to fasten in any sense, to join battle.
H631.kjv: bind, fast, gird, harness, hold, keep, make ready, order, prepare, prison(-er), put in bonds, set in array, tie

H632.def: or אִסָּר; from H631 (אָסַר); an obligation or vow (of abstinence).

H633.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H632 (אֱסָר) in a legal sense; an interdict.

H634.def: of foreign derivation; Esar-chaddon, an Assyrian king.

H635.def: of Persian derivation; Ester, the Jewish heroine.

H636.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H6086 (עֵץ); a tree or wood.

H637.gloss: meaning accession (used as an adverb or conjunction)
H637.def: a primitive particle; meaning accession (used as an adverb or conjunction); also or yea; adversatively though.
H637.kjv: also, although, and (furthermore, yet), but, even, how much less (more, rather than), moreover, with, yea

H638.gloss: meaning accession (used as an adverb or conjunction)
H638.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H637 (אַף); meaning accession (used as an adverb or conjunction); also or yea; adversatively though.

H639.def: from H599 (אָנַף); properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also (from the rapid breathing in passion) ire.
H639.kjv: anger(-gry), before, countenance, face, forebearing, forehead, (long-) suffering, nose, nostril, snout, worthy, wrath

H640.def: a primitive root (rather a denominative from H646 (אֵפוֹד)); to gird on (the ephod).

H641.def: the same as H646 (אֵפוֹד) shortened; Ephod, an Israelite.

H642.def: feminine of H646 (אֵפוֹד); a girding on (of the ephod); hence, generally, a plating (of metal).

H643.def: apparently of foreign derivation; a pavilion or palace-tent.

H644.def: a primitive root; to cook, especially to bake.

H645.def: or אֵפוֹא; from H6311 (פֹּה); strictly a demonstrative particle, here; but used of time, now or then.

H646.def: rarely אֵפֹד; probably of foreign derivation; a girdle; specifically the ephod or highpriest's shoulder-piece; also generally, an image.

H647.def: perhaps from H6315 (פּוּחַ); breeze; Aphiach, an Israelite.

H648.def: from the same as H651 (אָפֵל) (in the sense of weakness); unripe.

H649.def: dual of H639 (אַף); two nostrils; Appajim, an Israelite.

H650.def: from H622 (אָסַף); properly, containing, i.e. a tube; also a bed or valley of astream; also a strong thing or a hero.
H650.kjv: brook, channel, mighty, river, scale, stream, strong piece

H651.def: from an unused root meaning to set as the sun; dusky.

H652.def: from the same as H651 (אָפֵל); dusk.

H653.def: feminine of H651 (אָפֵל); duskiness, figuratively, misfortune; concrete, concealment.

H654.def: from H6419 (פָּלַל); judge; Ephlal, an Israelite.

H655.def: from an unused root meaning to revolve; a turn, i.e. a season.

H656.def: a primitive root; to disappear, i.e. cease.
H656.kjv: be clean gone (at an end, brought to nought), fail

H657.gloss: cessation, i.e. an end (especially of the earth)
H657.def: from H656 (אָפֵס); (sometimes like H6466 (פָּעַל)); cessation, i.e. an end (especially of the earth); often used adverb, no further; also the ankle (in the dual), as being the extremity of the leg or foot.
H657.kjv: ankle, but (only), end, howbeit, less than nothing, nevertheless (where), no, none (beside), not (any, -withstanding), thing of nought, save(-ing), there, uttermost part, want, without (cause)

H658.def: from H657 (אֶפֶס) and the plural of H1818 (דָּם); boundary of blood-drops; Ephes-Dammim, a place in Palestine.

H659.def: from an unused root probably meaning to breathe; properly, a breath, i.e. nothing.

H660.gloss: an asp or other venomous serpent
H660.def: from H659 (אֵפַע) (in the sense of hissing); an asp or other venomous serpent.

H662.def: a primitive root; to contain, i.e. (reflex.) abstain.

H663.gloss: Aphek (or Aphik), the name of three places in Palestine
H663.def: or אֲפִיק; from H662 (אָפַק) (in the sense of strength); fortress; Aphek (or Aphik), the name of three places in Palestine.

H664.def: feminine of H663 (אֲפֵק); fortress; Aphekah, a place in Palestine.

H665.def: from an unused root meaning to bestrew; ashes.

H666.def: from the same as H665 (אֵפֶר) (in the sense of covering); a turban.

H667.def: from H6524 (פָּרַח) (in the sense of bursting the shell); the brood of a bird.

H668.def: probably of Egyptian derivation; a palanquin.

H669.def: dual of masculine form of H672 (אֶפְרָת); double fruit; Ephrajim, a son of Joseph; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory.

H670.gloss: an Apherasite or inhabitant of an unknown region of Assyria
H670.def: (Aramaic) of foreign origin (only in the plural); an Apherasite or inhabitant of an unknown region of Assyria.

H671.gloss: an Apharsekite or Apharsathkite, an unknown Assyrian tribe
H671.def: (Aramaic) or אֲפַרְסַתְכַי; (Aramaic), of foreign origin (only in the plural); an Apharsekite or Apharsathkite, an unknown Assyrian tribe.

H672.def: or אֶפְרָתָה; from H6509 (פָּרָה); fruitfulness; Ephrath, another name for Bethlehem;.
H672.kjv: once (Psalm 132:6) perhaps for Ephraim; also of an Israelitish woman; Ephrath, Ephratah

H673.def: patrial from H672 (אֶפְרָת); an Ephrathite or an Ephraimite.

H674.def: (Aramaic) of Persian origin; revenue; others at the last.

H675.gloss: Etsbon, the name of two Israelites
H675.def: or אֶצְבֹּן; of uncertain derivation; Etsbon, the name of two Israelites.

H676.gloss: something to sieze with, i.e. a finger
H676.def: from the same as H6648 (צֶבַע) (in the sense of grasping); something to sieze with, i.e. a finger; by analogy, a toe.

H677.gloss: something to sieze with, i.e. a finger
H677.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H676 (אֶצְבַּע); something to sieze with, i.e. a finger; by analogy, a toe.

H678.def: from H680 (אָצַל) (in its secondary sense of separation); an extremity (Isaiah 41:9), also a noble.

H679.gloss: a joint of the hand (i.e. knuckle)
H679.def: from H680 (אָצַל) (in its primary sense of uniting); a joint of the hand (i.e. knuckle); also (according to some) a party-wall (Ezekiel 41:8).

H680.def: a primitive root; properly, to join; used only as a denominative from H681 (אֵצֶל); to separate; hence, to select, refuse, contract.

H681.def: from H680 (אָצַל) (in the sense of joining); a side; (as a preposition) near.
H681.kjv: at, (hard) by, (from) (beside), near (unto), toward, with. See also H1018 (בֵּית הָאֵצֶל)

H682.gloss: Atsel, the name of an Israelite, and of a place in Palestine
H682.def: from H680 (אָצַל); noble; Atsel, the name of an Israelite, and of a place in Palestine.

H683.def: from H680 (אָצַל) and H3050 (יָהּ) prolonged; Jah has reserved; Atsaljah, an Israelite.

H684.gloss: Otsem, the name of two Israelites
H684.def: from an unused root probably meaning to be strong; strength (i.e. strong); Otsem, the name of two Israelites.

H685.def: a variation from H6807 (צְעָדָה); properly, a step-chain; by analogy, a bracelet.

H686.def: a primitive root; to store up.

H687.def: from H686 (אָצַר); treasure; Etser, an Idumaean.

H688.gloss: burning, i.e. a carbuncle or other fiery gem
H688.def: from H6916 (קִדָּה); burning, i.e. a carbuncle or other fiery gem.

H689.def: probably from H602 (אָנַק); slender, i.e. the ibex.

H690.def: probably for H738 (אֲרִי); lion; Ara, an Israelite.

H692.def: from H691 (אֶרְאֵל); heroic; Areli (or an Arelite, collectively), an Israelite and his descendants.

H694.def: from H693 (אָרַב); ambush; Arab, a place in Palestine.

H697.def: from H7235 (רָבָה); a locust (from its rapid increase).

H698.def: feminine of H696 (אֹרֶב) (only in the plural); ambuscades.

H699.def: feminine participle passive of H693 (אָרַב) (as if for lurking); a lattice; (by implication) a window, dovecot (because of the pigeon-holes), chimney (with its apertures for smoke), sluice (with openings for water).

H700.def: plural of H699 (אֲרֻבָּה); Arubboth, a place in Palestine.

H701.gloss: an Arbite or native of Arab
H701.def: patrial from H694 (אֲרָב); an Arbite or native of Arab.

H704.def: the same as H702 (אַרְבַּע); Arba, one of the Anakim.

H707.def: a primitive root; to plait or weave.

H708.def: from H707 (אָרַג); a weaving; a braid; also a shuttle.

H709.def: from the same as H7263 (רֶגֶב); stony; Argob, a district of Palestine.

H712.def: perhaps from H7264 (רָגַז) (in the sense of being suspended),; a box (as a pannier).

H713.gloss: purple (the color or the dyed stuff)
