H713.def: of foreign origin; purple (the color or the dyed stuff).

H714.gloss: Ard, the name of two Israelites
H714.def: from an unused root probably meaning to wander; fugitive; Ard, the name of two Israelites.

H715.def: from the same as H714 (אַרְדְּ); roaming; Ardon, an Israelite.

H716.gloss: an Ardite (collectively) or descendant of Ard
H716.def: patronymic from H714 (אַרְדְּ); an Ardite (collectively) or descendant of Ard.

H719.def: probably from H7300 (רוּד); a refuge for the roving; Arvad, an island-city of Palestine.

H720.def: an orthographical variation of H719 (אַרְוַד); fugitive; Arod, an Israelite.

H721.gloss: an Arvadite or citizen of Arvad
H721.def: patrial from H719 (אַרְוַד); an Arvadite or citizen of Arvad.

H722.gloss: an Arodite or descendant of Arod
H722.def: patronymic from H721 (אַרְוָדִי); an Arodite or descendant of Arod.

H723.def: or אֲרָיָה; from H717 (אָרָה) (in the sense of feeding); a herding-place for an animal.

H724.def: or אֲרֻכָה; feminine passive participle of H748 (אָרַךְ) (in the sense of restoring to soundness); wholeness (literally or figuratively).

H725.def: a variation of H7316 (רוּמָה); height; Arumah, a place in Palestine.

H726.def: a clerical error for H130 (אֱדֹמִי); an Edomite (as in the margin).

H727.def: or אָרֹן; from H717 (אָרָה) (in the sense of gathering); a box.

H728.gloss: Aravnah (or Arnijah or Ornah), a Jebusite
H728.def: or (by transposition) אוֹרְנָה; or אַרְנִיָּהlemma אַנִיָּה missing resh, corrected to אַרְנִיָּה; all by orthographical variation for H771 (אׇרְנָן); Aravnah (or Arnijah or Ornah), a Jebusite.

H729.def: a primitive root; to be firm; used only in the passive participle as a denominative from H730 (אֶרֶז); of cedar.

H730.gloss: a cedar tree (from the tenacity of its roots)
H730.def: from H729 (אָרַז); a cedar tree (from the tenacity of its roots).

H733.gloss: Arach, the name of three Israelites
H733.def: from H732 (אָרַח); way faring; Arach, the name of three Israelites.

H734.def: from H732 (אָרַח); a well-trodden road (literally or figuratively); also a caravan.
H734.kjv: manner, path, race, rank, traveller, troop, (by-, high-) way

H736.def: feminine active participle of H732 (אָרַח); a caravan.

H737.def: feminine passive participle of H732 (אָרַח) (in the sense of appointing); a ration of food.

H738.def: or (prolonged) []; from H717 (אָרָה) (in the sense of violence); a lion.

H739.def: or אֲרִאֵל; from H738 (אֲרִי) and H410 (אֵל); lion of God; i.e. heroic.

H740.def: the same as H739 (אֲרִיאֵל); Ariel, a symbolical name for Jerusalem, also the name of an Israelite.

H741.def: either by transposition for H739 (אֲרִיאֵל) or, more probably, an orthographical variation for H2025 (הַרְאֵל); the altar of the temple.

H742.def: of Persian origin; Aridai, a son of Haman.

H743.def: of Persian origin; Aridatha, a son of Haman.

H745.def: the same as H738 (אֲרִי); lion; Arjeh, an Israelite.

H746.gloss: Arjok, the name of two Babylonians
H746.def: of foreign origin; Arjok, the name of two Babylonians.

H747.def: of Persian origin; Arisai, a son of Haman.

H748.gloss: to be (causative, make) long (literally or figuratively)
H748.def: a primitive root; to be (causative, make) long (literally or figuratively).
H748.kjv: defer, draw out, lengthen, (be, become, make, pro-) long, (out-, over-) live, tarry (long)

H749.def: (Aramaic) properly, corresponding to H748 (אָרַךְ), but used only in the sense of reaching to a given point; to suit.

H751.def: from H748 (אָרַךְ); length; Erek, a place in Babylon.

H755.def: (Aramaic) from an unused root corresponding to H7392 (רָכַב) (in the sense of bending the knee); the knee.

H756.gloss: an Arkevite (collectively) or native of Erek
H756.def: (Aramaic) patrial from H751 (אֶרֶךְ); an Arkevite (collectively) or native of Erek.

H757.gloss: an Arkite or native of Erek
H757.def: patrial from another place (in Palestine) of similar name with H751 (אֶרֶךְ); an Arkite or native of Erek.

H758.gloss: Aram or Syria, and its inhabitants
H758.def: from the same as H759 (אַרְמוֹן); the highland; Aram or Syria, and its inhabitants; also the name of the son of Shem, a grandson of Nahor, and of an Israelite.

H759.def: from an unused root (meaning to be elevated); a citadel (from its height).

H760.def: from H758 (אֲרָם) and H6678 (צוֹבָא); Aram of Tsoba (or Coele-Syria).

H761.def: patrial from H758 (אֲרָם); an Aramite or Aramaean.

H762.def: feminine of H761 (אֲרַמִּי); (only adverbial) in Aramean.
H762.kjv: in the Syrian language (tongue), in Syriac

H763.gloss: Aram of (the) two rivers (Euphrates and Tigris) or Mesopo
H763.def: from H758 (אֲרָם) and the dual of H5104 (נָהָר); Aram of (the) two rivers (Euphrates and Tigris) or Mesopotamia.

H764.def: from H759 (אַרְמוֹן); palatial; Armoni, an Israelite.

H765.def: from H7442 (רָנַן); stridulous; Aran, an Edomite.

H766.def: from the same as H765 (אֲרָן) (in the sense of strength); the ash tree (from its toughness).

H767.def: the same as H766 (אֹרֶן); Oren, an Israelite.

H769.gloss: the Arnon, a river east of the Jordan, also its territory
H769.def: or אַרְנֹןlemma אַרְנן missing vowel, corrected to אַרְנֹן; from H7442 (רָנַן); a brawling stream; the Arnon, a river east of the Jordan, also its territory.

H770.def: probably from the same as H769 (אַרְנוֹן); noisy; Arnan, an Israelite.

H771.def: probably from H766 (אֹרֶן); strong; Ornan, a Jebusite.

H772.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H776 (אֶרֶץ); the earth; by implication (figuratively) low.

H774.def: from H7502 (רָפַד); spread out; Arpad, a place in Syria.

H775.def: probably of foreign origin; Arpakshad, a son of Noah; also the region settled by him.

H776.def: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth (at large, or partitively a land).

H777.def: from H776 (אֶרֶץ); earthiness; Artsa, an Israelite.

H778.def: (Aramaic) by transmutation for H772 (אֲרַע); the earth.

H780.def: of foreign origin; Ararat (or rather Armenia).

H781.def: a primitive root; to engage for matrimony.

H782.def: from H781 (אָרַשׂ) (in the sense of desiring to possess); a longing for.

H783.def: or אַרְתַּחְשַׁשְׁתְּא; or by permutation אַרְתַּחְשַׁסְתְּא; of foreign origin; Artachshasta (or Artaxerxes), a title (rather than name) of several Persian kings.

H784.def: a primitive word; fire (literally or figuratively).

H785.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H784 (אֵשׁ); fire (literally or figuratively).

H786.gloss: entity, used only adverbially, there is or are
H786.def: identical (in origin and formation) with H784 (אֵשׁ); entity, used only adverbially, there is or are.
H786.kjv: are there, none can. Compare H3426 (יֵשׁ)

H787.def: (Aramaic) corresponding (by transposition and abbreviation) to H803 (אֲשׁוּיָה); a foundation.

H788.def: probably from the same as H7640 (שֹׁבֶל); flowing; Ashbel, an Israelite.

H789.gloss: an Ashbelite (collectively) or descendant of Ashbel
H789.def: patronymic from H788 (אַשְׁבֵּל); an Ashbelite (collectively) or descendant of Ashbel.

H790.def: probably from the same as H7644 (שֶׁבְנָא); vigorous; Eshban, an Idumaean.

H791.def: from H7650 (שָׁבַע); adjurer; Asbea, an Israelite.

H792.gloss: Eshbaal (or Ishbosheth), a son of Saul
H792.def: from H376 (אִישׁ) and H1168 (בַּעַל); man of Baal; Eshbaal (or Ishbosheth), a son of Saul.

H793.def: from an unused root meaning to pour; an outpouring.

H795.def: from H7703 (שָׁדַד); ravager; Ashdod, a place in Palestine.

H796.gloss: an Ashdodite (often collectively) or inhabitant of Asdod
H796.def: patrial from H795 (אַשְׁדּוֹד); an Ashdodite (often collectively) or inhabitant of Asdod.

H797.gloss: (only adverb) in the language of Ashdod
H797.def: feminine of H796 (אַשְׁדּוֹדִי); (only adverb) in the language of Ashdod.

H798.gloss: Ashdoth-Pisgah, a place east of the Jordan
H798.def: from the plural of H794 (אֲשֵׁדָה) and H6449 (פִּסְגָּה) with the article interposed; ravines of the Pisgah; Ashdoth-Pisgah, a place east of the Jordan.

H799.def: from H784 (אֵשׁ) and H1881 (דָּת); a fire-law.

H801.def: the same as H800 (אֶשָּׁה), but used in a liturgical sense; properly, a burnt-offering; but occasionally of any sacrifice.

H802.def: feminine of H376 (אִישׁ) or H582 (אֱנוֹשׁ); irregular plural, נָשִׁים;(used in the same wide sense as H582 (אֱנוֹשׁ)); a woman.
H802.kjv: (adulter) ess, each, every, female, many, none, one, together, wife, woman. Often unexpressed in English

H803.def: feminine passive participle from an unused root meaning to found; foundation.

H804.gloss: Ashshur, the second son of Shem
H804.def: or אַשֻּׁר; apparently from H833 (אָשַׁר) (in the sense of successful); Ashshur, the second son of Shem; also his descendants and the country occupied by them (i.e. Assyria), its region and its empire.
H804.kjv: Asshur, Assur, Assyria, Assyrians. See H838 (אָשֻׁר)

H805.gloss: an Ashurite (collectively) or inhabitant of Ashur
H805.def: or אַשּׁוּרִי; from a patrial word of the same form as H804 (אַשּׁוּר); an Ashurite (collectively) or inhabitant of Ashur, a district in Palestine.

H806.def: probably from H7835 (שָׁחַר); black; Ashchur, an Israelite.

H807.def: of foreign origin; Ashima, a deity of Hamath.

H808.def: from the same as H784 (אֵשׁ) (in the sense of pressing down firmly; compare H803 (אֲשׁוּיָה)); a (ruined) foundation.

H809.def: feminine of H808 (אָשִׁישׁ); something closely pressed together, i.e. a cake of raisins or other comfits.

H810.def: from an unused root (probably meaning to bunch together); a testicle (as a lump).

H811.gloss: a bunch of grapes or other fruit
H811.def: or אֶשְׁכֹּל; probably prolonged from H810 (אֶשֶׁךְ); a bunch of grapes or other fruit.

H812.def: the same as H811 (אֶשְׁכּוֹל); Eshcol, the name of an Amorite, also of a valley in Palestine.

H813.gloss: Ashkenaz, a Japhethite, also his descendants
H813.def: of foreign origin; Ashkenaz, a Japhethite, also his descendants.

H815.def: from a root of uncertain signification; a tamarisk tree; by extension, a grove of any kind.

H816.def: or אָשֵׁם; a primitive root; to be guilty; by implication to be punished or perish.
H816.kjv: certainly, be(-come, made) desolate, destroy, greatly, be(-come, found, hold) guilty, offend (acknowledge offence), trespass

H817.def: from H816 (אָשַׁם); guilt; by implication, a fault; also a sin-offering.

H818.def: from H816 (אָשַׁם); guilty; hence, presenting asin-offering.

H819.gloss: guiltiness, a fault, the presentation of asin-offering
H819.def: feminine of H817 (אָשָׁם); guiltiness, a fault, the presentation of asin-offering.

H821.def: or אַשׁמוּרָה; or אַשׁמֹרֶת; (feminine) from H8104 (שָׁמַר); a night watch.

H822.def: apparently from an unused root (probably meaning to leave interstices); a latticed window.

H823.gloss: Ashnah, the name of two places in Palestine
H823.def: probably a variation for H3466 (יְשָׁנָה); Ashnah, the name of two places in Palestine.

H824.def: from H8172 (שָׁעַן); support; Eshan, a place in Palestine.

H825.def: from an unused root (probably meaning to lisp, i.e. practice enchantment); a conjurer.

H827.def: perhaps (feminine) from the same as H825 (אַשָּׁף) (in the sense of covering); a quiver or arrow-case.

H828.def: of foreign origin; Ashpenaz, a Babylonian eunuch.

H829.def: of uncertain derivation; a measured portion.

H830.gloss: a heap of rubbish or filth
H830.def: or אַשְׁפּוֹת; or שְׁפֹת; (contraction) plural of a noun of the same form as H827 (אַשְׁפָּה), from H8192 (שָׁפָה) (in the sense of scraping); a heap of rubbish or filth.

H831.def: probably from H8254 (שָׁקַל) in the sense of weighing-place (i.e. mart); Ashkelon, a place in Palestine.

H832.def: patrial from H831 (אַשְׁקְלוֹן); Ashkelonite (collectively) or inhabitant of Ashkelon.

H833.gloss: to be straight (used in the widest sense
H833.def: or אָשֵׁר; a primitive root; to be straight (used in the widest sense, especially to be level, right, happy); figuratively, to go forward, be honest, proper.
H833.kjv: (call, be) bless(-ed, happy), go, guide, lead, relieve

H834.def: a primitive relative pronoun (of every gender and number); who, which, what, that; also (as an adverb and a conjunction) when, where, how, because, in order that, etc..
H834.kjv: after, alike, as (soon as), because, every, for, forasmuch, from whence, how(-soever), if, (so) that ((thing) which, wherein), though, until, whatsoever, when, where (-as, -in, -of, -on, -soever, -with), which, whilst, whither(-soever), who(-m, -soever, -se). As it is indeclinable, it is often accompanied by the personal pronoun expletively, used to show the connection

H835.def: from H833 (אָשַׁר); happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!.

H836.def: from H833 (אָשַׁר),; happy; Asher, a son of Jacob, and the tribe descended from him, with its territory; also a place in Palestine.

H838.def: or אַשֻּׁר; from H833 (אָשַׁר) in the sense of going; a step.

H839.gloss: the cedar tree or some other light elastic wood
H839.def: contracted for H8391 (תְּאַשּׁוּר); the cedar tree or some other light elastic wood.

H840.def: by orthographical variation from H833 (אָשַׁר) and H410 (אֵל); right of God; Asarel, an Israelite.

H841.def: from the same as H840 (אֲשַׂרְאֵל); right toward God; Asarelah, an Israelite.

H842.def: or אֲשֵׁירָה; from H833 (אָשַׁר); happy; Asherah (or Astarte) a Phoenician goddess; also an image of the same.

H843.gloss: an Asherite (collectively) or descendant of Asher
H843.def: patronymic from H836 (אָשֵׁר); an Asherite (collectively) or descendant of Asher.

H844.gloss: Asriel, the name of two Israelites
H844.def: an orthographical variation for H840 (אֲשַׂרְאֵל); Asriel, the name of two Israelites.

H845.gloss: an Asrielite (collectively) or descendant of Asriel
H845.def: patronymic from H844 (אַשְׂרִיאֵל); an Asrielite (collectively) or descendant of Asriel.

H846.def: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H833 (אָשַׁר); a wall (from its uprightness).

H847.def: or אֶשְׁתָּאוֹל; probably from H7592 (שָׁאַל); intreaty; Eshtaol, a place in Palestine.

H848.gloss: an Eshtaolite (collectively) or inhabitant of Eshtaol
H848.def: patrial from H847 (אֶשְׁתָּאֹל); an Eshtaolite (collectively) or inhabitant of Eshtaol.

H849.def: (Aramaic) from H7712 (שְׁדַר) (in a bad sense); rebellion.

H850.def: probably from the same as H7764 (שׁוּנִי); restful; Eshton, an Israelite.

H851.gloss: Eshtemoa or Eshtemoh, a place in Palestine
H851.def: or אֶשְׁתְּמוֹעַ; or אֶשְׁתְּמֹה; from H8085 (שָׁמַע) (in the sense of obedience); Eshtemoa or Eshtemoh, a place in Palestine.

H853.gloss: self (but generally used to point out more definitely the
H853.def: apparent contracted from H226 (אוֹת) in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self (but generally used to point out more definitely the object of a verb or preposition, even or namely).

H854.gloss: nearness (used only as a preposition or an adverb), near
H854.def: probably from H579 (אָנָה); properly, nearness (used only as a preposition or an adverb), near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc..
H854.kjv: against, among, before, by, for, from, in(-to), (out) of, with. Often with another prepositional prefix

H855.gloss: a hoe or other digging implement
H855.def: of uncertain derivation; a hoe or other digging implement.

H856.def: from H854 (אֵת) and H1168 (בַּעַל); with Baal; Ethbaal, a Phoenician king.

H857.def: or אָתָא; a primitive root (collateral to H225 (אוּת) contraction); to arrive.

H858.def: (Aramaic) or אָתָא; (Aramaic), corresponding to H857 (אָתָה); to arrive.

H859.gloss: thou and thee, or (plural) ye and you
H859.def: or (shortened); אַתָּ; or אַת; feminine (irregular) sometimes אַתִּי; plural masculine אַתֶּם; feminine אַתֶּן; or אַתֵּנָהlemma אתֵּנָה missing vowel, corrected to אַתֵּנָה; or אַתֵּנָּה; a primitive pronoun of the second person; thou and thee, or (plural) ye and you.

H860.def: probably from the same as H386 (אֵיתָן) (in the sense of patience); a female donkey (from its docility).

H861.def: (Aramaic) probably from the corresponding to H784 (אֵשׁ); probably a fire-place, i.e. furnace.

H862.gloss: a ledge or offset in abuilding
H862.def: or אַתִּיק; from H5423 (נָתַק) in the sense of decreasing; a ledge or offset in abuilding.

H863.gloss: Ittai or Ithai, the name of a Gittite and of an Israelite
H863.def: or אִיתַי; from H854 (אֵת); near; Ittai or Ithai, the name of a Gittite and of an Israelite.

H864.gloss: Etham, a place in the Desert
H864.def: of Egyptian derivation; Etham, a place in the Desert.

H865.def: or אִתְמוֹל; or אֶתְמוּל; probably from H853 (אֵת) or H854 (אֵת) and H4136 (מוּל); heretofore; definitely yesterday.
H865.kjv: before (that) time, heretofore, of late (old), times past, yester(day)

H866.gloss: a present (as the price of harlotry)
H866.def: from H8566 (תָּנָה); a present (as the price of harlotry).

H867.def: perhaps from H866 (אֶתְנָה); munificence; Ethni, an Israelite.

H868.gloss: a gift (as the price of harlotry or idolatry)
H868.def: the same as H866 (אֶתְנָה); a gift (as the price of harlotry or idolatry).

H869.def: the same as H868 (אֶתְנַן) in the sense of H867 (אֶתְנִי); Ethnan, an Israelite.

H870.def: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to that of H871 (אֲתָרִים); a place; (adverb) after.

H871.def: plural from an unused root (probably meaning to step); places; Atharim, a place near Palestine.

H872.def: from H935 (בּוֹא); an entrance to a building.

H874.def: a primitive root; to dig; by analogy, to engrave; figuratively, to explain.

H875.def: from H874 (בָּאַר); a pit; especially a well.

H876.gloss: Beer, a place in the Desert, also one in Palestine
H876.def: the same as H875 (בְּאֵר); Beer, a place in the Desert, also one in Palestine.

H878.def: from H875 (בְּאֵר); a well; Beera, an Israelite.

H879.gloss: Beer-Elim, a place in the Desert
H879.def: from H875 (בְּאֵר) and the plural of H410 (אֵל); well of heroes; Beer-Elim, a place in the Desert.

H880.def: the same as H878 (בְּאֵרָא); Beerah, an Israelite.

H881.def: feminine plural of H875 (בְּאֵר); wells; Beeroth, a place in Palestine.

H882.gloss: Beeri, the name of a Hittite and of an Israelite
H882.def: from H875 (בְּאֵר); fountained; Beeri, the name of a Hittite and of an Israelite.

H883.gloss: Beer-Lachai-Roi, a place in the Desert
H883.def: from H875 (בְּאֵר) and H2416 (חַי) (with prefix) and H7203 (רֹאֶה); well of a living (One) my Seer; Beer-Lachai-Roi, a place in the Desert.

H884.def: from H875 (בְּאֵר) and H7651 (שֶׁבַע) (in the sense of H7650 (שָׁבַע)); well of an oath; Beer-Sheba, a place in Palestine.

H885.gloss: Beeroth-Bene-Jaakan, a place in the Desert
H885.def: from the feminine plural of H875 (בְּאֵר), and the plural contraction of H1121 (בֵּן), and H3292 (יַעֲקָן); wells of (the) sons of Jaakan; Beeroth-Bene-Jaakan, a place in the Desert.
H885.kjv: Beeroth of the children of Jaakan

H886.gloss: a Beerothite or inhabitant of Beeroth
H886.def: patrial from H881 (בְּאֵרוֹת); a Beerothite or inhabitant of Beeroth.

H887.def: a primitive root; to smell bad; figuratively, to be offensive morally.
H887.kjv: (make to) be abhorred (had in abomination, loathsome, odious), (cause a, make to) stink(-ing savour), utterly

H888.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H887 (בָּאַשׁ); to smell bad; figuratively, to be offensive morally.

H890.gloss: stink-weed or any other noxious or useless plant
H890.def: feminine of H889 (בְּאֹשׁ); stink-weed or any other noxious or useless plant.

H892.gloss: something hollowed (as a gate), i.e. pupil of the eye
H892.def: feminine active participle of an unused root meaning to hollow out; something hollowed (as a gate), i.e. pupil of the eye.

H893.def: probably of foreign origin; Bebai, an Israelite.

H894.def: from H1101 (בָּלַל); confusion; Babel (i.e. Babylon), including Babylonia and the Babylonian empire.

H895.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H894 (בָּבֶל); Babel (i.e. Babylon), including Babylonia and the Babylonian empire.

H898.def: a primitive root; to cover (with a garment); figuratively, to act covertly; by implication, to pillage.
H898.kjv: deal deceitfully (treacherously, unfaithfully), offend, transgress(-or), (depart), treacherous (dealer, -ly, man), unfaithful(-ly, man), very

H899.def: from H898 (בָּגַד); a covering, i.e. clothing; also treachery or pillage.
H899.kjv: apparel, cloth(-es, ing), garment, lap, rag, raiment, robe, very (treacherously), vesture, wardrobe

H900.def: feminine plural active participle of H898 (בָּגַד); treacheries.

H902.def: probably of foreign origin; Bigvai, an Israelite.

H903.def: of Persian derivation; Bigtha, a eunuch of Xerxes.

H904.gloss: Bigthan or Bigthana, a eunuch of Xerxes
H904.def: or בִּגְתָנָא; of similar derivation to H903 (בִּגְתָא); Bigthan or Bigthana, a eunuch of Xerxes.

H905.def: from H909 (בָּדַד); properly, separation; by implication, a part of the body, branch of atree, bar forcarrying; figuratively, chief of a city; especially (with prepositional prefix) as an adverb, apart, only, besides.
H905.kjv: alone, apart, bar, besides, branch, by self, of each alike, except, only, part, staff, strength

H906.def: perhaps from H909 (בָּדַד) (in the sense of divided fibres); flaxen thread or yarn; hence, a linen garment.

H907.def: from H908 (בָּדָא); a brag or lie; also a liar.

H909.def: a primitive root; to divide, i.e. (reflex.) be solitary.

H911.def: from H909 (בָּדַד); separation; Bedad, an Edomite.

H912.def: probably a shortened form H5662 (עֹבַדְיָה); servant of Jehovah; Bedejah, an Israelite.

H913.def: from H914 (בָּדַל); alloy (because removed by smelting); by analogy, tin.

H914.gloss: to divide (in variation senses literally or figuratively
H914.def: a primitive root; to divide (in variation senses literally or figuratively, separate, distinguish, differ, select, etc.).
H914.kjv: (make, put) difference, divide (asunder), (make) separate (self, -ation), sever (out), utterly

H916.def: probably from H914 (בָּדַל); something in pieces, i.e. bdellium, a (fragrant) gum (perhaps amber); others a pearl.

H917.gloss: Bedan, the name of two Israelites
H917.def: probably shortened for H5658 (עַבְדוֹן); servile; Bedan, the name of two Israelites.

H918.def: a primitive root; to gap open; used only as a denominative from H919 (בֶּדֶק); to mend a breach.

H919.gloss: a gap or leak (in a building or a ship)
H919.def: from H918 (בָּדַק); a gap or leak (in a building or a ship).

H920.def: probably from H1856 (דָּקַר) with a prepositional prefix; by stabbing, i.e. assassin; Bidkar, an Israelite.

H921.def: (Aramaic) corresponding (by transposition) to H6504 (פָּרַד); to scatter.

H922.gloss: a vacuity, i.e. (superficially) an undistinguishable ruin
H922.def: from an unused root (meaning to be empty); a vacuity, i.e. (superficially) an undistinguishable ruin.

H923.def: from an unused root (probably meaning to glisten); white marble or perhaps alabaster.

H924.def: (Aramaic) from H927 (בְּהַל); a hurry; only adverb, hastily.

H925.def: from an unused root (meaning to be bright); shining.

H926.def: a primitive root; to tremble inwardly (or palpitate), i.e. (figuratively) be (causative, make) (suddenly) alarmed or agitated; by implication to hasten anxiously.
H926.kjv: be (make) affrighted (afraid, amazed, dismayed, rash), (be, get, make) haste(-n, -y, -ily), (give) speedy(-ily), thrust out, trouble, vex

H927.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H926 (בָּהַל); to terrify, hasten.

H929.def: from an unused root (probably meaning to be mute); properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal (often collective).

H930.gloss: a water-ox, i.e. the hippopotamus or Nile-horse
H930.def: in form a plural or H929 (בְּהֵמָה), but really a singular of Egyptian derivation; a water-ox, i.e. the hippopotamus or Nile-horse.

H931.gloss: the thumb of the hand or great toe of the foot
H931.def: from an unused root apparently meaning to be thick; the thumb of the hand or great toe of the foot.

H932.def: an orthographical variation of H931 (בֹּהֶן); thumb, Bohan, an Israelite.

H933.def: from an unused root meaning to be pale; white scurf.

H934.gloss: a whitish spot on the skin
H934.def: feminine active participle of the same as H925 (בָּהִיר); a whitish spot on the skin.

H935.gloss: to go or come (in a wide variety of applications)
H935.def: a primitive root; to go or come (in a wide variety of applications).
H935.kjv: abide, apply, attain, be, befall, besiege, bring (forth, in, into, to pass), call, carry, certainly, (cause, let, thing for) to come (against, in, out, upon, to pass), depart, doubtless again, eat, employ, (cause to) enter (in, into, -tering, -trance, -try), be fallen, fetch, follow, get, give, go (down, in, to war), grant, have, indeed, (in-) vade, lead, lift (up), mention, pull in, put, resort, run (down), send, set, (well) stricken (in age), surely, take (in), way

H938.gloss: Buz, the name of a son of Nahor, and of an Israelite
H938.def: the same as H937 (בּוּז); Buz, the name of a son of Nahor, and of an Israelite.

H939.def: feminine passive participle of H936 (בּוּז); something scorned; an object of contempt.

H940.gloss: a Buzite or descendant of Buz
H940.def: patronymic from H938 (בּוּז); a Buzite or descendant of Buz.

H941.def: the same as H940 (בּוּזִי); Buzi, an Israelite.

H942.def: probably of Persian origin; Bavvai, an Israelite.

H943.def: a primitive root; to involve (literally or figuratively).

H944.def: for H2981 (יְבוּל); produce (of the earth, etc.).

H945.def: the same as H944 (בּוּל) (in the sense of rain); Bul, the eighth Hebrew month.

H946.def: from H995 (בִּין); discretion; Bunah, an Israelite.

H947.def: a primitive root; to trample (literally or figuratively).

H948.def: from an unused root (of the same form) meaning to bleach, i.e. (intransitive) be white; probably cotton (of some sort).

H949.def: from the same as H948 (בּוּץ); shining; Botsets, a rock near Michmash.

H950.def: feminine passive participle of an unused root (meaning to be hollow); emptiness (as adjective).

H951.def: properly, active participle from H1239 (בָּקַר) as denominative from H1241 (בָּקָר); a cattle-tender.

H952.def: a primitive root; to bore, i.e. (figuratively) examine.

H953.gloss: a pit hole (especially one used as a cistern or a prison)
H953.def: from H952 (בּוּר) (in the sense of H877 (בֹּאר)); a pit hole (especially one used as a cistern or a prison).

H954.gloss: to pale, i.e. by implication to be ashamed
H954.def: a primitive root; properly, to pale, i.e. by implication to be ashamed; also (by implication) to be disappointed or delayed.
H954.kjv: (be, make, bring to, cause, put to, with, a-) shamed(-d), be (put to) confounded(-fusion), become dry, delay, be long

H955.def: feminine participle passive of H954 (בּוּשׁ); shame.

H956.def: (Aramaic) apparent denominative from H1005 (בַּיִת); to lodge over night.

H958.def: a primitive root; probably to cleave.

H959.kjv: despise, disdain, contemn(-ptible), think to scorn, vile person

H962.kjv: catch, gather, (take) for a prey, rob(-ber), spoil, take (away, spoil), utterly

H964.def: from H959 (בָּזָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); contempts of Jah; Bizjothjah, a place in Palestine.

H965.def: from an unused root meaning to lighten; a flash of lightning.

H966.def: from H965 (בָּזָק); lightning; Bezek, a place in Palestine.

H968.def: of Persian origin; Biztha, a eunuch of Xerxes.

H969.def: from H974 (בָּחַן); an assayer or metals.

H970.def: or בָּחֻר; participle passive of H977 (בָּחַר); properly, selected, i.e. a youth (often collective).

H971.def: another form of H975 (בַּחַן); a watch-tower of besiegers.

H973.kjv: abhor, get hastily (from the margin for H926 (בָּהַל))

H974.def: a primitive root; to test (especially metals); generally and figuratively, to investigate.

H975.def: from H974 (בָּחַן) (in the sense of keeping a look-out); a watch-tower.

H977.def: a primitive root; properly, to try, i.e. (by implication) select.
H977.kjv: acceptable, appoint, choose (choice), excellent, join, be rather, require

H978.gloss: a Bacharumite or inhabitant of Bachurim
H978.def: patrial from H980 (בַּחֻרִים) (by transposition); a Bacharumite or inhabitant of Bachurim.

H979.def: or בְּחוּרוֹת; feminine plural of H970 (בָּחוּר); also (masculine plural) בְּחֻרִים; youth (collectively and abstractly).

H980.def: or בַּחוּרִים; masculine plural of H970 (בָּחוּר); young men; Bachurim, a place in Palestine.

H981.def: or בָּטָה; a primitive root; to babble; hence, to vociferate angrily.

H982.gloss: figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure
H982.def: a primitive root; properly, to hide for refuge (but not so precipitately as H2620 (חָסָה)); figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
H982.kjv: be bold (confident, secure, sure), careless (one, woman), put confidence, (make to) hope, (put, make to) trust

H983.def: from H982 (בָּטַח); properly, a place of refuge; abstract, safety, both the fact (security) and the feeling (trust); often (adverb with or without preposition) safely.
H983.kjv: assurance, boldly, (without) care(-less), confidence, hope, safe(-ly, -ty), secure, surely

H984.def: the same as H983 (בֶּטַח); Betach, a place in Syria.

H988.def: a primitive root; to desist from labor.

H990.def: from an unused root probably meaning to be hollow; the belly, especially the womb; also the bosom or body of anything.
H990.kjv: belly, body, as they be born, within, womb

H991.def: the same as H990 (בֶּטֶן); Beten. a place in Palestine.

H992.gloss: (only in plural) a pistachio-nut (from its form)
H992.def: from H990 (בֶּטֶן); (only in plural) a pistachio-nut (from its form).

H993.def: probably plural from H992 (בֹּטֶן); hollows; Betonim, a place in Palestine.

H994.def: perhaps from H1158 (בָּעָה) (in the sense of asking); properly, a request; used only adverbially (always with 'my Lord'); oh that!; with leave, or if it please.

H995.def: a primitive root; to separate mentally (or distinguish), i.e.(generally) understand.
H995.kjv: attend, consider, be cunning, diligently, direct, discern, eloquent, feel, inform, instruct, have intelligence, know, look well to, mark, perceive, be prudent, regard, (can) skill(-full), teach, think, (cause, make to, get, give, have) understand(-ing), view, (deal) wise(-ly, man)

H996.def: (sometimes in the plural masculine or feminine); properly, the constructive form of an otherwise unused noun from H995 (בִּין); a distinction; but used only as a preposition,; between (repeated before each noun, often with other particles); also as a conjunction, either...or.
H996.kjv: among, asunder, at, between (-twixt...and), from (the widest), in, out of, whether (it be...or), within

H997.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H996 (בֵּין); between (repeated before each noun, often with other particles); also as a conjunction, either...or.

H1000.def: from the same as H948 (בּוּץ); an egg (from its whiteness).

H1002.def: of foreign origin; a castle or palace.

H1004.gloss: a house (in the greatest variation of applications
H1004.def: probably from H1129 (בָּנָה) abbreviated; a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.).
H1004.kjv: court, daughter, door, dungeon, family, forth of, great as would contain, hangings, home(born), (winter) house(-hold), inside(-ward), palace, place, prison, steward, tablet, temple, web, within(-out)

H1005.gloss: a house (in the greatest variation of applications
H1005.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1004 (בַּיִת); a house (in the greatest variation of applications, especially family, etc.).

H1006.def: the same as H1004 (בַּיִת); Bajith, a place in Palestine.

H1007.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H205 (אָוֶן); house of vanity; Beth-Aven, a place in Palestine.

H1008.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H410 (אֵל); house of God; Beth-El, a place in Palestine.

H1009.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H695 (אֶרֶב) and H410 (אֵל); house of God's ambush; Beth-Arbel, a place in Palestine.

H1010.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1168 (בַּעַל) and H4583 (מָעוֹן); house of Baal of (the) habitation of (apparently by transposition); or (shorter) בֵּית מְעוֹןlemma בֵּית מעוֹן missing vowel, corrected to בֵּית מְעוֹן; house of habitation of (Baal); Beth-Baal-Meon, a place in Palestine.
H1010.kjv: Beth-baal-meon. Compare H1186 (בַּעַל מְעוֹן) and H1194 (בְּעֹן)

H1011.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1254 (בָּרָא); house of a creative one; Beth-Biri, a place in Palestine.

H1012.def: probably from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H5679 (עֲבָרָה); house of (the) ford; Beth-Barah, a place in Palestine.

H1013.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1447 (גָּדֵר); house of (the) wall; Beth-Gader, a place in Palestine.

H1014.gloss: Beth-Gamul, a place East of the Jordan
H1014.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the passive participle of H1576 (גְּמוּל); house of (the) weaned; Beth-Gamul, a place East of the Jordan.

H1015.gloss: Beth-Diblathajim, a place East of the Jordan
H1015.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the dual of H1690 (דְּבֵלָה); house of (the) two figcakes; Beth-Diblathajim, a place East of the Jordan.

H1016.gloss: Beth-Dagon, the name of two places in Palestine
H1016.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1712 (דָּגוֹן); house of Dagon; Beth-Dagon, the name of two places in Palestine.

H1017.gloss: a Beth-elite, or inhabitant of Bethel
H1017.def: patrial from H1008 (בֵּית־אֵל) with the article interposed; a Beth-elite, or inhabitant of Bethel.

H1018.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H681 (אֵצֶל) with the article interposed; house of the side; Beth-ha-Etsel, a place in Palestine.

H1019.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H1537 (גִּלְגָּל) with the article interposed; house of Gilgal (or rolling); Beth-hag-Gilgal, a place in Palestine.

H1020.gloss: Beth-ha-Jeshimoth, a town East of the Jordan
H1020.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the plural of H3451 (יְשִׁימַה) with the article interposed; house of the deserts; Beth-ha-Jeshimoth, a town East of the Jordan.

H1021.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H3754 (כֶּרֶם) with the article interposed; house of the vineyard; Beth-hak-Kerem, a place in Palestine.

H1022.gloss: a Beth-lechemite, or native of Bethlechem
H1022.def: patrial from H1035 (בֵּית לֶחֶם) with the article inserted; a Beth-lechemite, or native of Bethlechem.

H1023.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4801 (מֶרְחָק) with the article interposed; house of the breadth; Beth-ham-Merchak, a place in Palestine.

H1024.gloss: Beth-ham-Markaboth or Beth-Markaboth, a place in Palestine
H1024.def: or (shortened) בֵּית מַרְכָּבוֹת; from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the plural of H4818 (מֶרְכָּבָה) (with or without the article interposed); place of (the) chariots; Beth-ham-Markaboth or Beth-Markaboth, a place in Palestine.

H1025.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6010 (עֵמֶק) with the article interposed; house of the valley; Beth-ha-Emek, a place in Palestine.

H1026.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6160 (עֲרָבָה) with the article interposed; house of the Desert; Beth-ha-Arabah, a place in Palestine.

H1027.gloss: Beth-ha-Ram, a place East of the Jordan
H1027.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7311 (רוּם) with the article interposed; house of the height; Beth-ha-Ram, a place East of the Jordan.

H1028.gloss: Beth-ha-Ram, a place East of the Jordan
H1028.def: probably for H1027 (בֵּית הָרָם); Beth-ha-Ram, a place East of the Jordan.

H1029.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7848 (שִׁטָּה) with the article interposed; house of the acacia; Beth-hash-Shittah, a place in Palestine.

H1030.gloss: a Beth-shimshite, or inhabitant of Bethshemesh
H1030.def: patrial from H1053 (בֵּית שֶׁמֶשׁ) with the article inserted; a Beth-shimshite, or inhabitant of Bethshemesh.

H1031.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the same as H2295 (חׇגְלָה); house of a partridge; Beth-Choglah, a place in Palestine.

H1032.gloss: Beth-Choron, the name of two adjoining places in Palestine
H1032.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H2356 (חוֹר); house of hollowness; Beth-Choron, the name of two adjoining places in Palestine.

H1033.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H3733 (כַּר); house of pasture; Beth-Car, a place in Palestine.

H1034.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the plural of H3833 (לָבִיא); house of lionesses; Beth-Lebaoth, a place in Palestine.

H1035.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H3899 (לֶחֶם); house of bread; Beth-Lechem, a place in Palestine.

H1036.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the feminine of H6083 (עָפָר) (with preposition interposed); house to (i.e. of) dust; Beth-le-Aphrah, a place in Palestine.

H1037.gloss: Beth-Millo, the name of two citadels
H1037.def: or בֵּית מִלֹּא; from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4407 (מִלּוֹא); house of (the) rampart; Beth-Millo, the name of two citadels.

H1038.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H4601 (מַעֲכָה); house of Maakah; Beth-Maakah, a place in Palestine.

H1039.gloss: Beth-Nimrah, a place east of the Jordan
H1039.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and the feminine of H5246 (נָמֵר); house of (the) leopard; Beth-Nimrah, a place east of the Jordan.

H1040.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H5730 (עֵדֶן); house of pleasure; Beth-Eden, a place in Syria.

H1041.gloss: house of Azmaveth, a place in Palestine
H1041.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H5820 (עַזְמָוֶת); house of Azmaveth, a place in Palestine.

H1042.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and a plural from H6030 (עָנָה); house of replies; Beth-Anoth, a place in Palestine.

H1043.def: an orthographical variation for H1042 (בֵּית עֲנוֹת); Beth-Anath, a place in Palestine.

H1044.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and a derivative of H6123 (עָקַד); house of (the) binding (for sheep-shearing); Beth-Eked, a place in Palestine.

H1045.def: ; from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6252 (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת); house of Ashtoreths; Beth-Ashtaroth, a place in Palestine.
H1045.kjv: house of Ashtaroth. Compare H1203 (בְּעֶשְׁתְּרָה), H6252 (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת)

H1046.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6412 (פָּלִיט); house of escape; Beth-Palet, a place in Palestine.

H1047.gloss: Beth-Peor, a place East of the Jordan
H1047.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6465 (פְּעוֹר); house of Peor; Beth-Peor, a place East of the Jordan.

H1048.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and a derivative from H6327 (פּוּץ); house of dispersion; Beth-Patstsets, a place in Palestine.

H1049.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H6697 (צוּר); house of (the) rock; Beth-Tsur, a place in Palestine.

H1050.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7339 (רְחֹב); house of (the) street; Beth-Rechob, a place in Palestine.

H1051.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7497 (רָפָא); house of (the) giant; Beth-Rapha, an Israelite.

H1052.gloss: Beth-Shean or Beth-Shan, a place in Palestine
H1052.def: or בֵּית שָׁן; from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H7599 (שָׁאַן); house of ease; Beth-Shean or Beth-Shan, a place in Palestine.

H1053.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H8121 (שֶׁמֶשׁ); house of (the) sun; Beth-Shemesh, a place in Palestine.

H1054.def: from H1004 (בַּיִת) and H8598 (תַּפּוּחַ); house of (the) apple; Beth-Tappuach, a place in Palestine.

H1055.def: probably from H1004 (בַּיִת); a palace (i.e. large house).

H1056.def: from H1058 (בָּכָה), weeping; Baca, a valley in Palestine.

H1057.def: the same as H1056 (בָּכָא); the weeping tree (some gum-distilling tree, perhaps the balsam).

H1058.def: a primitive root; to weep; generally to bemoan.
H1058.kjv: at all, bewail, complain, make lamentation, more, mourn, sore, with tears, weep

H1061.def: from H1069 (בָּכַר); the first-fruits of the crop.

H1062.gloss: the firstling of man or beast
H1062.def: or (short) בְּכֹרָה; feminine of H1060 (בְּכוֹר); the firstling of man or beast; abstractly primogeniture.

H1063.def: feminine of H1061 (בִּכּוּר); the early fig.

H1064.def: feminine of H1062 (בְּכוֹרָה); primogeniture; Bekorath, an Israelite.

H1065.def: from H1058 (בָּכָה); a weeping; by analogy, a dripping.

H1066.def: plural active participle of H1058 (בָּכָה); (with the article) the weepers; Bo-kim, a place in Palestine.

H1067.def: feminine from H1069 (בָּכַר); the eldest daughter.

H1069.def: a primitive root; properly, to burst the womb, i.e. (causatively) bear or make early fruit (of woman or tree); also (as denominative from H1061 (בִּכּוּר)); to give the birthright.
H1069.kjv: make firstborn, be firstling, bring forth first child (new fruit)

H1070.def: from H1069 (בָּכַר) (in the sense of youth); a young camel.

H1071.gloss: Beker, the name of two Israelites
H1071.def: the same as H1070 (בֶּכֶר); Beker, the name of two Israelites.

H1072.def: feminine of H1070 (בֶּכֶר); a young she-camel.

H1073.def: by orthographical variation for H1063 (בִּכּוּרָה); a first-ripe fig.

H1074.def: from H1069 (בָּכַר); first-born; Bokeru, an Israelite.

H1075.def: from H1069 (בָּכַר); youth-ful; Bikri, an Israelite.

H1076.gloss: a Bakrite (collectively) or descendants of Beker
H1076.def: patronymically from H1071 (בֶכֶר); a Bakrite (collectively) or descendants of Beker.

H1077.def: from H1086 (בָּלָה); properly, a failure; by implication nothing; usually (adverb) not at all; also lest.
H1077.kjv: lest, neither, no, none (that...), not (any), nothing

H1078.gloss: Bel, the Baal of the Babylonians
H1078.def: by contraction for H1168 (בַּעַל); Bel, the Baal of the Babylonians.

H1079.gloss: anxiety, i.e. (by implication) the heart (as its seat)
H1079.def: (Aramaic) from H1080 (בְּלָא); properly, anxiety, i.e. (by implication) the heart (as its seat).

H1080.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1086 (בָּלָה) (but used only in a mental sense); to afflict.

H1081.gloss: Baladan, the name of a Babylonian prince
H1081.def: from H1078 (בֵּל) and H113 (אָדוֹן) (contracted); Bel (is his) lord; Baladan, the name of a Babylonian prince.

H1082.gloss: to break off or loose (in a favorable or unfavorable sense)
H1082.def: a primitive root; to break off or loose (in a favorable or unfavorable sense), i.e. desist (from grief) or invade (with destruction).

H1083.gloss: Bilgah, the name of two Israelites
H1083.def: from H1082 (בָּלַג); desistance; Bilgah, the name of two Israelites.

H1084.def: from H1082 (בָּלַג); desistant; Bilgai, an Israelite.

H1085.def: of uncertain derivation; Bildad, one of Job's friends.

H1086.def: a primitive root; to fail; by implication to wear out, decay (causatively, consume, spend).
H1086.kjv: consume, enjoy long, become (make, wax) old, spend, waste

H1088.def: feminine of H1087 (בָּלֶה); failure; Balah, a place in Palestine.

H1089.def: a primitive root (rather by transposition for H926 (בָּהַל)); to palpitate; hence, (causatively) to terrify.

H1090.gloss: Bilhah, the name of one of Jacob's concubines
H1090.def: from H1089 (בָּלַהּ); timid; Bilhah, the name of one of Jacob's concubines; also of a place in Palestine.

H1092.gloss: Bilhan, the name of an Edomite and of an Israelite
H1092.def: from H1089 (בָּלַהּ); timid; Bilhan, the name of an Edomite and of an Israelite.

H1093.def: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H1086 (בָּלָה); excise (on articles consumed).

H1094.def: or (fully) בְּלוֹי; from H1086 (בָּלָה); (only in plural construction) rags.

H1095.gloss: Belteshatstsar, the Babylonian name of Daniel
H1095.def: of foreign derivation; Belteshatstsar, the Babylonian name of Daniel.

H1096.gloss: Belteshatstsar, the Babylonian name of Daniel
H1096.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1095 (בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּר); Belteshatstsar, the Babylonian name of Daniel.

H1097.def: from H1086 (בָּלָה); properly, failure, i.e. nothing or destruction; usually (with preposition) without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc..
H1097.kjv: corruption, ig(norantly), for lack of, where no...is, so that no, none, not, un(awares), without

H1098.def: from H1101 (בָּלַל); mixed, i.e. (specifically) feed (for cattle).

H1099.def: from H1097 (בְּלִי) and H4100 (מָה); (as indefinitely) nothing whatever.

H1100.def: from H1097 (בְּלִי) and H3276 (יַעַל);(often in connection with H376 (אִישׁ), H802 (אִשָּׁה), H1121 (בֵּן), etc.); without profit, worthlessness; by extension, destruction, wickedness.

H1101.def: a primitive root; (also  denominatively from H1098 (בְּלִיל)) to fodder; to overflow (specifically with oil.); by implication, to mix; to fodder.
H1101.kjv: anoint, confound, fade, mingle, mix (self), give provender, temper

H1103.gloss: to pinch sycamore figs (a process necessary to ripen them)
H1103.def: a primitive root; to pinch sycamore figs (a process necessary to ripen them).

H1104.gloss: to make away with (specifically by swallowing)
H1104.def: a primitive root; to make away with (specifically by swallowing); generally, to destroy.
H1104.kjv: cover, destroy, devour, eat up, be at end, spend up, swallow down (up)

H1105.def: from H1104 (בָּלַע); a gulp; figuratively, destruction.
H1105.kjv: devouring, that which he hath swallowed up

H1106.def: the same as H1105 (בֶּלַע); Bela, the name of a place, also of an Edomite and of two Israelites.

H1107.def: or בַּלְעֲדֵי; constructive plural from H1077 (בַּל) and H5703 (עַד), not till, i.e. (as preposition or adverb); except, without, besides.

H1108.gloss: a Belaite (collectively) or descendants of Bela
H1108.def: patronymically from H1106 (בֶּלַע); a Belaite (collectively) or descendants of Bela.

H1109.def: probably from H1077 (בַּל) and H5971 (עַם); not (of the) people, i.e. foreigner; Bilam, a Mesopotamian prophet; also a place in Palestine.

H1111.def: from H1110 (בָּלַק); waster; Balak, a Moabitish king.

H1112.def: or בֵּלְאשַׁצַּרlemma בֵּלְאשַׁצּר missing vowel, corrected to בֵּלְאשַׁצַּר; of foreign origin (compare H1095 (בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּר)); Belshatstsar, a Babylonian king.

H1113.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1112 (בֵּלְשַׁאצַּר); Belshatstsar, a Babylonian king.

H1114.def: of uncertain derivation; Bilshan, an Israelite.

H1115.def: constructive feminine of H1086 (בָּלָה) (equivalent to H1097 (בְּלִי)); properly, a failure of, i.e. (used only as a negative particle, usually with a prepositional prefix) not, except, without, unless, besides, because not, until, etc..
H1115.kjv: because un(satiable), beside, but, continual, except, from, lest, neither, no more, none, not, nothing, save, that no, without

H1116.def: from an unused root (meaning to be high); an elevation.

H1117.def: the same as H1116 (בָּמָה); Bamah, a place in Palestine.

H1118.def: probably from H4107 (מָהַל) with prepositional prefix; with pruning; Bimhal, an Israelite.

H1119.def: prolongation for prepositional prefix; in, with, by, etc..

H1120.gloss: Bamoth or Bamoth-Baal, a place East of the Jordan
H1120.def: plural of H1116 (בָּמָה); heights; or (fully) בָּמוֹת בַּעַל; from the same and H1168 (בַּעַל); heights of Baal; Bamoth or Bamoth-Baal, a place East of the Jordan.

H1121.def: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship).

H1122.def: the same as H1121 (בֵּן); Ben, an Israelite.

H1123.gloss: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense
H1123.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1121 (בֵּן); a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense.

H1124.def: (Aramaic) or בְּנָה; (Aramaic), corresponding to H1129 (בָּנָה); to build.

H1125.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H40 (אֲבִימֶלֶךְ); (the) son of Abinadab; Ben-Abinadab, an Israelite.

H1126.gloss: Ben-Oni, the original name of Benjamin
H1126.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H205 (אָוֶן); son of my sorrow; Ben-Oni, the original name of Benjamin.

H1127.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H1397 (גֶּבֶר); son of (the) hero; Ben-Geber, an Israelite.

H1128.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and a derivative of H1856 (דָּקַר); son of piercing (or of a lance); Ben-Deker, an Israelite.

H1129.def: a primitive root; to build (literally and figuratively).
H1129.kjv: (begin to) build(-er), obtain children, make, repair, set (up), surely

H1130.gloss: Ben-Hadad, the name of several Syrian kings
H1130.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H1908 (הֲדַד); son of Hadad; Ben-Hadad, the name of several Syrian kings.

H1131.def: from H1129 (בָּנָה); built up; Binnui, an Israelite.

H1132.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2105 (זוֹחֵת); son of Zocheth; Ben-Zocheth, an Israelite.

H1133.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2354 (חוּר); son of Chur; Ben-Chur, an Israelite.

H1134.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2428 (חַיִל); son of might; Ben-Chail, an Israelite.

H1135.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2605 (חָנָן); son of Chanan; Ben-Chanan, an Israelite.

H1136.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H2617 (חֵסֵד); son of kindness; Ben-Chesed, an Israelite.

H1137.gloss: Bani, the name of five Israelites
H1137.def: from H1129 (בָּנָה); built; Bani, the name of five Israelites.

H1138.def: or (fuller) בּוּנִי; from H1129 (בָּנָה); built; Bunni or Buni, an Israelite.

H1139.gloss: sons of lightning, Bene-berak, a place in Palestine
H1139.def: from the plural construction of H1121 (בֵּן) and H1300 (בָּרָק); sons of lightning, Bene-berak, a place in Palestine.

H1141.gloss: Benajah, the name of twelve Israelites
H1141.def: or בּנָיָהוּ; (prolonged) from H1129 (בָּנָה) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah has built; Benajah, the name of twelve Israelites.

H1142.gloss: Bene-Jaakan, a place in the Desert
H1142.def: from the plural of H1121 (בֵּן) and H3292 (יַעֲקָן); sons of Yaakan; Bene-Jaakan, a place in the Desert.

H1143.gloss: a double interval, i.e. the space between two armies
H1143.def: dual of H996 (בֵּין); a double interval, i.e. the space between two armies.

H1144.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H3225 (יָמִין); son of (the) right hand; Binjamin, youngest son of Jacob; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory.

H1145.gloss: a Benjaminite, or descendent of Benjamin
H1145.def: sometimes (with the article inserted) בֵּן־הַיּמִינִי; with H376 (אִישׁ) inserted (1 Samuel 9:1) בֶּן־אִישׁ ימִינִי; son of a man of Jemini; or shortened אִישׁ יְמִינִי; (1 Samuel 9:4; Esther 2:5) a man of Jemini, or (1 Samuel 20:1) simply יְמִינִיxlit Yᵉmînîy corrected to Yᵉmîynîy; a Jeminite; (plural) בְּנֵי יְמִינִיxlit Bᵉnîy corrected to Bᵉnêy; patron from H1144 (בִּנְיָמִין); a Benjaminite, or descendent of Benjamin.

H1148.def: probably from H1121 (בֵּן) with pron. suff.; our son; Beninu, an Israelite.

H1149.def: (Aramaic) of uncertain affinity; to be enraged.

H1150.def: or בִּנְעָה; of uncertain derivation; Bina or Binah, an Israelite.

H1151.def: from H1121 (בֵּן) and H5971 (עַם) with pronomial suffix; son of my people; Ben-Ammi, a son of Lot.

H1152.def: from H5475 (סוֹד) and H3050 (יָהּ) with prepositional prefix; in (the) counsel of Jehovah; Besodejah, an Israelite.

H1153.def: from H947 (בּוּס); domineering; Besai, one of the Nethinim.

H1154.def: from an unused root meaning to be sour; an immature grape.

H1155.def: from the same as H1154 (בֶּסֶר); an immature grape.

H1156.def: (Aramaic) or בְּעָה; (Aramaic), corresponding to H1158 (בָּעָה); to seek or ask.
H1156.kjv: ask, desire, make (petition), pray, request, seek

H1157.gloss: in up to or over against
H1157.def: from H5704 (עַד) with prepositional prefix; in up to or over against; generally at, beside, among, behind, for, etc..
H1157.kjv: about, at by (means of), for, over, through, up (-on), within

H1158.gloss: to gush over, i.e. to swell
H1158.def: a primitive root; to gush over, i.e. to swell; (figuratively) to desire earnestly; by implication to ask.
H1158.kjv: cause, inquire, seek up, swell out

H1160.gloss: Beor, the name of the father of an Edomitish king
H1160.def: from H1197 (בָּעַר) (in the sense of burning); a lamp; Beor, the name of the father of an Edomitish king; also of that of Balaam.

H1162.def: from an unused root of uncertain meaning; Boaz, the ancestor of David; also the name of a pillar in front of the temple.

H1163.def: a primitive root; to trample down, i.e. (figuratively) despise.

H1165.def: from H1197 (בָּעַר) (in the sense of eating); cattle.

H1166.def: a primitive root; also as denominative from H1167 (בַּעַל); to be master; hence, to marry.
H1166.kjv: have dominion (over), be husband, marry(-ried, wife)

H1167.def: from H1166 (בָּעַל); a master; hence, a husband, or (figuratively) owner (often used with another noun in modifications of this latter sense).
H1167.kjv: archer, babbler, bird, captain, chief man, confederate, have to do, dreamer, those to whom it is due, furious, those that are given to it, great, hairy, he that hath it, have, horseman, husband, lord, man, married, master, person, sworn, they of

H1168.def: the same as H1167 (בַּעַל); Baal, a Phoenician deity.

H1169.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1167 (בַּעַל); a master; hence, a husband,.

H1170.gloss: Baal-Berith, a special deity of the Shechemites
H1170.def: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H1285 (בְּרִית); Baal of (the) covenant; Baal-Berith, a special deity of the Shechemites.

H1171.def: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H1409 (גָּד); Baal of Fortune; Baal-Gad, a place in Syria.

H1173.gloss: Baalah, the name of three places in Palestine
H1173.def: the same as H1172 (בַּעֲלָה); Baalah, the name of three places in Palestine.

H1174.def: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H1995 (הָמוֹן); possessor of a multitude; Baal-Hamon, a place in Palestine.

H1175.def: plural of H1172 (בַּעֲלָה); mistresses; Bealoth, a place in Palestine.
H1175.kjv: Bealoth, in Aloth (by mistake for a plural from H5927 (עָלָה) with prepositional prefix)

H1176.gloss: Baal-Zebub, a special deity of the Ekronites
H1176.def: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H2070 (זְבוּב); Baal of (the) Fly; Baal-Zebub, a special deity of the Ekronites.

H1177.gloss: Baal-Chanan, the name of an Edomite, also of an Israelite
H1177.def: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H2603 (חָנַן); possessor of grace; Baal-Chanan, the name of an Edomite, also of an Israelite.

H1178.def: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and a modification of H2691 (חָצֵר); possessor of a village; Baal-Chatsor, a place in Palestine.

H1179.def: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H2768 (חֶרְמוֹן); possessor of Hermon; Baal-Chermon, a place in Palestine.

H1180.gloss: Baali, a symbolical name for Jehovah
H1180.def: from H1167 (בַּעַל) with pron. suff.; my master; Baali, a symbolical name for Jehovah.

H1181.gloss: Baale-Bamoth, a place East of the Jordan
H1181.def: from the plural of H1168 (בַּעַל) and the plural of H1116 (בָּמָה); Baals of (the) heights; Baale-Bamoth, a place East of the Jordan.

H1182.def: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H3045 (יָדַע); Baal has known; Beeljada, an Israelite.

H1183.def: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H3050 (יָהּ); Jah (is) master; Bealjah, an Israelite.

H1184.def: from the plural of H1167 (בַּעַל) and H3063 (יְהוּדָה); masters of Judah; Baale-Jehudah, a place in Palestine.

H1185.def: probably from a derivative of H5965 (עָלַס) with prepositional prefix; in exultation; Baalis, an Ammonitish king.

H1186.gloss: Baal-Meon, a place East of the Jordan
H1186.def: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H4583 (מָעוֹן); Baal of (the) habitation (of) (compare H1010 (בֵּית בַּעַל מְעוֹן)); Baal-Meon, a place East of the Jordan.

H1187.def: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H6465 (פְּעוֹר); Baal of Peor; Baal-Peor, a Moabitish deity.

H1188.def: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and the plural of H6556 (פֶּרֶץ); possessor of breaches; Baal-Peratsim, a place in Palestine.

H1189.def: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H6828 (צָפוֹן) (in the sense of cold) (according to others an Egyptian form of Typhon, the destroyer); Baal of winter; Baal-Tsephon, a place in Eqypt.

H1190.gloss: Baal of Shalishah, Baal-Shalishah, a place in Palestine
H1190.def: from H1168 (בַּעַל) and H8031 (שָׁלִשָׁה); Baal of Shalishah, Baal-Shalishah, a place in Palestine.

H1191.def: a modification of H1172 (בַּעֲלָה); mistressship; Baalath, a place in Palestine.

H1192.def: from H1172 (בַּעֲלָה) and H875 (בְּאֵר); mistress of a well; Baalath-Beer, a place in Palestine.

H1193.def: from H1167 (בַּעַל) and H8558 (תָּמָר); possessor of (the) palm-tree; Baal-Tamar, a place in Palestine.

H1194.gloss: Beon, a place East of the Jordan
H1194.def: probably a contraction of H1010 (בֵּית בַּעַל מְעוֹן); Beon, a place East of the Jordan.

H1195.gloss: Banana, the name of four Israelite
H1195.def: the same as H1196 (בַּעֲנָה); Banana, the name of four Israelite.

H1196.gloss: Baanah, the name of four Israelites
H1196.def: from a derivative of H6031 (עָנָה) with prepositional prefix; in affliction; Baanah, the name of four Israelites.

H1197.gloss: to kindle, i.e. consume (by fire or by eating)
H1197.def: a primitive root; also as denominative from H1198 (בַּעַר); to kindle, i.e. consume (by fire or by eating);  to be(-come) brutish.
H1197.kjv: be brutish, bring (put, take) away, burn, (cause to) eat (up), feed, heat, kindle, set (on fire), waste

H1198.def: from H1197 (בָּעַר); properly, foot (as consumed); i.e. (by exten.) of cattle brutishness; (concretely) stupid.

H1199.def: from H1198 (בַּעַר); brutish; Baara, an Israelitish woman.

H1201.def: from an unused root meaning to stink; offensiveness; Basha, a king of Israel.

H1202.def: from H6213 (עָשָׂה) and H3050 (יָהּ) with a prepositional prefix; in (the) work of Jah; Baasejah, an Israelite.

H1203.gloss: Beeshterah, a place East of the Jordan
H1203.def: from H6251 (עַשְׁתְּרָה) (as singular of H6252 (עַשְׁתָּרוֹת)) with a prepositional prefix; with Ashtoreth; Beeshterah, a place East of the Jordan.

H1206.def: probably the same as H948 (בּוּץ); mud (as whitish clay).

H1209.gloss: Betsai, the name of two Israelites
H1209.def: perhaps the same as H1153 (בְּסַי); Betsai, the name of two Israelites.

H1210.def: from H1219 (בָּצַר); clipped, i.e. the grape crop.

H1211.def: from an unused root apparently meaning to peel; an onion.

H1212.gloss: Betsalel, the name of two Israelites
H1212.def: probably from H6738 (צֵל) and H410 (אֵל) with a prepositional prefix; in (the) shadow (i.e. protection) of God; Betsalel, the name of two Israelites.

H1213.def: or בַּצְלִית; from the same as H1211 (בֶּצֶל)xlit bᵉtsel corrected to betsel; a peeling; Batsluth or Batslith, an Israelite.

H1214.def: a primitive root to break off, i.e. (usually) plunder; figuratively, to finish, or (intransitively) stop.
H1214.kjv: (be) covet(-ous), cut (off), finish, fulfill, gain (greedily), get, be given to (covetousness), greedy, perform, be wounded

H1215.def: from H1214 (בָּצַע); plunder; by extension, gain (usually unjust).

H1216.gloss: perhaps to swell up, i.e. blister
H1216.def: a primitive root; perhaps to swell up, i.e. blister.

H1217.def: from H1216 (בָּצֵק); dough (as swelling by fermentation).

H1218.def: from H1216 (בָּצֵק); a swell of ground; Botscath, a place in Palestine.

H1219.def: a primitive root; to clip off; specifically (as denominative from H1210 (בָּצִיר)); to gather grapes; also to be isolated (i.e. inaccessible by height or fortification).
H1219.kjv: cut off, (de-) fenced, fortify, (grape) gather(-er), mighty things, restrain, strong, wall (up), withhold

H1220.gloss: strictly a clipping, i.e. gold (as dug out)
H1220.def: from H1219 (בָּצַר); strictly a clipping, i.e. gold (as dug out).

H1221.def: the same as H1220 (בֶּצֶר), an inaccessible spot; Betser, a place in Palestine; also an Israelite.

H1223.def: feminine from H1219 (בָּצַר); an enclosure, i.e. sheep fold.

H1224.def: the same as H1223 (בׇּצְרָה); Botsrah, a place in Edom.

H1225.def: masculine intensive from H1219 (בָּצַר); a fortress.

H1226.def: feminine intensive from H1219 (בָּצַר); restraint (of rain), i.e. drought.

H1227.def: the same as H1228 (בַּקְבֻּק)xlit baqbûk corrected to baqbuq; Bakbuk, one of the Nethinim.

H1228.gloss: a bottle (from the gurgling in emptying)
H1228.def: from H1238 (בָּקַק); a bottle (from the gurgling in emptying).

H1229.def: from H1228 (בַּקְבֻּק)xlit baqbûk corrected to baqbuq and H3050 (יָהּ); emptying (i.e. wasting) of Jah; Bakbukjah, an Israelite.

H1230.def: reduplicated from H1239 (בָּקַר); searcher; Bakbakkar, an Israelite.

H1231.gloss: Bukki, the name of two Israelites
H1231.def: from H1238 (בָּקַק); wasteful; Bukki, the name of two Israelites.

H1232.def: from H1238 (בָּקַק) and H3050 (יָהּ); wasting of Jah; Bukkijah, an Israelite.

H1234.def: a primitive root; to cleave; generally, to rend, break, rip or open.
H1234.kjv: make a breach, break forth (into, out, in pieces, through, up), be ready to burst, cleave (asunder), cut out, divide, hatch, rend (asunder), rip up, tear, win

H1235.def: from H1234 (בָּקַע); a section (half) of ashekel, i.e. a beka (a weight and a coin).

H1236.gloss: a split, i.e. a wide level valley between mountains
H1236.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1237 (בִּקְעָה); properly, a split, i.e. a wide level valley between mountains.

H1237.gloss: a split, i.e. a wide level valley between mountains
H1237.def: from H1234 (בָּקַע); properly, a split, i.e. a wide level valley between mountains.

H1238.gloss: to pour out, i.e. to empty, figuratively, to depopulate
H1238.def: a primitive root; to pour out, i.e. to empty, figuratively, to depopulate; by analogy, to spread out (as a fruitful vine).

H1239.def: a primitive root; properly, to plough, or (generally) break forth, i.e. (figuratively) to inspect, admire, care for, consider.

H1240.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1239 (בָּקַר); properly, to plough, or (generally) break forth, i.e. (figuratively) to inspect, admire, care for, consider.

H1241.gloss: beef cattle or an animal of the ox family of either gende
H1241.def: from H1239 (בָּקַר); beef cattle or an animal of the ox family of either gender (as used for plowing); collectively, a herd.
H1241.kjv: beeve, bull (-ock), calf, cow, great (cattle), heifer, herd, kine, ox

H1242.def: properly, dawn (as the break of day); generally, morning.

H1243.def: intensive from H1239 (בָּקַר); a looking after.

H1244.def: from H1239 (בָּקַר); properly, examination, i.e. (by implication) punishment.

H1245.def: a primitive root; to search out (by any method, specifically in worship or prayer); by implication, to strive after.
H1245.kjv: ask, beg, beseech, desire, enquire, get, make inquisition, procure, (make) request, require, seek (for)

H1247.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1121 (בֵּן); a son, grandson, etc..

H1248.def: borrowed (as a title) from H1247 (בַּר); the heir (apparent to the throne).

H1249.def: from H1305 (בָּרַר) (in its various senses); beloved; also pure, empty.

H1250.gloss: grain of any kind (even while standing in the field)
H1250.def: or בַּר; from H1305 (בָּרַר) (in the sense of winnowing); grain of any kind (even while standing in the field); by extension the open country.

H1253.def: the same as H1252 (בֹּר); vegetable lye (from its cleansing); used as a soap forwashing, or a flux formetals.

H1254.def: a primitive root; (absolutely) to create; (qualified) to cut down (a wood), select, feed (as formative processes).

H1255.def: a variation of H4757 (מְרֹאדַךְ בַּלְאָדָן); Berodak-Baladan, a Babylonian king.

H1256.def: from H1254 (בָּרָא) and H8050 (שְׁמוּאֵל); Jah has created; Berajah, an Israelite.

H1257.def: by reduplication from H1250 (בָּר); a fowl (as fattened on grain).

H1260.def: from H1258 (בָּרַד); hail; Bered, the name of a place south of Palestine, also of an Israelite.

H1261.def: from H1258 (בָּרַד); spotted (as if with hail).

H1262.def: a primitive root; to select; also (as denominative from H1250 (בָּר)) to feed; also (as equivalent to H1305 (בָּרַר)) to render clear (Ecclesiastes 3:18); to select; to feed; to render clear.

H1263.gloss: Baruk, the name of three Israelites
H1263.def: passive participle from H1288 (בָרַךְ); blessed; Baruk, the name of three Israelites.

H1264.def: probably of foreign origin; damask (stuff of variegated thread).

H1265.def: of uncertain derivation; a cypress (?) tree; hence, a lance or a musical instrument (as made of that wood).

H1266.def: a variation of H1265 (בְּרוֹשׁ); the cypress (or some elastic tree).

H1268.gloss: Berothah or Berothai, a place north of Palestine
H1268.def: or בֵּרֹתַי; probably from H1266 (בְּרוֹת)lemma בֻּרוֹת first vowel, corrected to בְּרוֹת; cypress or cypresslike; Berothah or Berothai, a place north of Palestine.

H1269.def: probably feminine plural from an unused root (apparently mean to pierce); holes; Birzoth, an Israelite.

H1270.def: perhaps from the root of H1269 (בִּרְזוֹת); iron (as cutting); by extension, an iron implement.

H1271.gloss: Barzillai, the name of three Israelites
H1271.def: from H1270 (בַּרְזֶל); iron-hearted; Barzillai, the name of three Israelites.

H1272.gloss: to bolt, i.e. figuratively, to flee suddenly
H1272.def: a primitive root; to bolt, i.e. figuratively, to flee suddenly.
H1272.kjv: chase (away); drive away, fain, flee (away), put to flight, make haste, reach, run away, shoot

H1273.gloss: a Barchumite, or native of Bachurim
H1273.def: by transposition for H978 (בַּחֲרוּמִי); a Barchumite, or native of Bachurim.

H1275.def: probably by contraction from H882 (בְּאֵרִי); Beri, an Israelite.

H1276.gloss: (only in the plural and with the article) the Berites
H1276.def: of uncertain derivation; (only in the plural and with the article) the Berites, a place in Palestine.

H1277.def: from H1254 (בָּרָא) (in the sense of H1262 (בָּרָה)); fatted or plump.

H1278.def: feminine from H1254 (בָּרָא); a creation, i.e. a novelty.

H1281.def: or בָּרִחַ; (shortened) from H1272 (בָּרַח); a fugitive, i.e. the serpent (as fleeing), and the constellation by that name.

H1282.def: the same as H1281 (בָּרִיחַ); Bariach, an Israelite.

H1283.gloss: Beriah, the name of four Israelites
H1283.def: apparently from the feminine of H7451 (רַע) with a prepositional prefix; in trouble; Beriah, the name of four Israelites.

H1284.gloss: a Beriite (collectively) or descendants of Beriah
H1284.def: patronymically from H1283 (בְּרִיעָה); a Beriite (collectively) or descendants of Beriah.

H1285.gloss: a compact (because made by passing between pieces of flesh)
H1285.def: from H1262 (בָּרָה) (in the sense of cutting (like H1254 (בָּרָא))); a compact (because made by passing between pieces of flesh).

H1286.def: the same as H1285 (בְּרִית); Berith, a Shechemitish deity.

H1288.def: a primitive root; to kneel; by implication to bless God (as an act of adoration), and (vice-versa) man.

H1289.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1288 (בָרַךְ); to kneel; by implication to bless God (as an act of adoration), and (vice-versa) man (as a benefit); also (by euphemism) to curse (God or the king, as treason).

H1292.gloss: Barakel, the father of one of Job's friends
H1292.def: from H1288 (בָרַךְ) and H410 (אֵל), God has blessed; Barakel, the father of one of Job's friends.

H1293.def: from H1288 (בָרַךְ); benediction; by implication prosperity.

H1294.def: the same as H1293 (בְּרָכָה); Berakah, the name of an Israelite, and also of a valley in Palestine.

H1295.gloss: a reservoir (at which camels kneel as a resting-place)
H1295.def: from H1288 (בָרַךְ); a reservoir (at which camels kneel as a resting-place).

H1296.gloss: Berekjah, the name of six Israelites
H1296.def: or בֶּרֶכְיָהוּ; from H1290 (בֶּרֶךְ) and H3050 (יָהּ); knee (i.e. blessing) of Jah; Berekjah, the name of six Israelites.

H1297.def: (Aramaic) perhaps from H7313 (רוּם) with a prepositional prefix; properly, highly, i.e. surely; but used adversatively, however.

H1298.def: of uncertain derivation; Bera, a Sodomitish king.

H1300.def: from H1299 (בָּרַק); lightning; by analogy, a gleam; concretely, a flashing sword.

H1301.def: the same as H1300 (בָּרָק); Barak, an Israelite.

H1302.def: of uncertain derivation; Barkos, one of the Nethimim.

H1303.def: from H1300 (בָּרָק); a thorn (perhaps as burning brightly).

H1304.gloss: a gem (as flashing), perhaps the emerald
H1304.def: or בָּרְקַתxlit bârᵉkath corrected to bârᵉqath; from H1300 (בָּרָק); a gem (as flashing), perhaps the emerald.

H1305.def: a primitive root; to clarify (i.e. brighten), examine, select.
H1305.kjv: make bright, choice, chosen, cleanse (be clean), clearly, polished, (shew self) pure(-ify), purge (out)

H1306.def: probably from H7562 (רֶשַׁע) with a prepositional prefix; with wickedness; Birsha, a king of Gomorrah.

H1307.gloss: a Berothite, or inhabitant of Berothai
H1307.def: patrial from H1268 (בֵּרוֹתָה); a Berothite, or inhabitant of Berothai.

H1308.def: from H1319 (בָּשַׂר); cheerful; Besor, a stream of Palestine.

H1309.def: or (shortened) בְּשֹׂרָה; feminine from H1319 (בָּשַׂר); glad tidings; by implication, reward forgood news.

H1310.def: a primitive root; properly, to boil up; hence, to be done in cooking; figuratively to ripen.
H1310.kjv: bake, boil, bring forth, roast, seethe, sod (be sodden)

H1312.def: of foreign derivation; Bishlam, a Persian.

H1313.def: from an unused root meaning to be fragrant; (compare H5561 (סַם)); the balsam plant.

H1314.def: or בֹּשֶׂם; from the same as H1313 (בָּשָׂם); fragrance; by implication, spicery; also the balsam plant.

H1315.def: feminine of H1314 (בֶּשֶׂם) (the second form); fragrance; Bosmath, the name of a wife of Esau, and of a daughter of Solomon.

H1316.gloss: Bashan (often with the article), a region East of the Jordan
H1316.def: of uncertain derivation; Bashan (often with the article), a region East of the Jordan.

H1318.def: a primitive root; to trample down.

H1319.gloss: to be fresh, i.e. full (rosy, (figuratively) cheerful)
H1319.def: a primitive root; properly, to be fresh, i.e. full (rosy, (figuratively) cheerful); to announce (glad news).
H1319.kjv: messenger, preach, publish, shew forth, (bear, bring, carry, preach, good, tell good) tidings

H1320.def: from H1319 (בָּשַׂר); flesh (from its freshness); by extension, body, person; also (by euphemistically) the pudenda of aman.
H1320.kjv: body, (fat, lean) flesh(-ed), kin, (man-) kind, nakedness, self, skin

H1321.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1320 (בָּשָׂר); flesh (from its freshness); by extension, body, person; also (by euphemistically) the pudenda of aman.

H1322.gloss: shame (the feeling and the condition, as well as its cause)
H1322.def: from H954 (בּוּשׁ); shame (the feeling and the condition, as well as its cause); by implication (specifically) an idol.
H1322.kjv: ashamed, confusion, greatly, (put to) shame(-ful thing)

H1323.gloss: a daughter (used in the same wide sense as other terms of
H1323.def: from H1129 (בָּנָה) (as feminine of H1121 (בֵּן)); a daughter (used in the same wide sense as other terms of relationship, literally and figuratively).
H1323.kjv: apple (of the eye), branch, company, daughter, first, old, owl, town, village

H1324.gloss: a bath or Hebrew measure (as a means of division) of liquids
H1324.def: probably from the same as H1327 (בַּתָּה); a bath or Hebrew measure (as a means of division) of liquids.

H1325.gloss: a bath or Hebrew measure (as a means of division) of liquids
H1325.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1324 (בַּת); a bath or Hebrew measure (as a means of division) of liquids.

H1326.def: probably an orthographical variation for H1327 (בַּתָּה); desolation.

H1327.def: feminine from an unused root (meaning to break in pieces); desolation.

H1328.def: apparently from the same as H1326 (בָּתָה) and H410 (אֵל); destroyed of God; Bethuel, the name of a nephew of Abraham, and of a place in Palestine.

H1329.gloss: Bethul (i.e. Bethuel), a place in Palestine
H1329.def: for H1328 (בְּתוּאֵל); Bethul (i.e. Bethuel), a place in Palestine.

H1330.def: feminine passive participle of an unused root meaning to separate; a virgin (from her privacy); sometimes (by continuation) a bride; also (figuratively) a city or state.

H1331.def: masculine plural of the same as H1330 (בְּתוּלָה); (collectively and abstractly) virginity; by implication and concretely, the tokens of it.

H1332.def: from H1323 (בַּת) and H3050 (יָהּ); daughter (i.e. worshipper) of Jah; Bithjah, an Egyptian woman.

H1333.def: a primitive root; to cut in pieces.

H1334.def: a primitive root,; to chop up.

H1336.def: the same as H1335 (בֶּתֶר); Bether, a (craggy) place in Palestine.

H1337.def: from H1323 (בַּת) and a masculine plural from H7227 (רַב); the daughter (i.e. city) of Rabbah.

H1338.gloss: Bithron, a place East of the Jordan
H1338.def: from H1334 (בָּתַר); (with the article) the craggy spot; Bithron, a place East of the Jordan.

H1339.def: from H1323 (בַּת) and H7651 (שֶׁבַע) (in the sense of H7650 (שָׁבַע)); daughter of an oath; Bath-Sheba, the mother of Solomon.

H1340.def: from H1323 (בַּת) and H7771 (שׁוֹעַ); daughter of wealth;  the same as H1339 (בַּת־שֶׁבַע); Bath-shua,.

H1342.def: a primitive root; to mount up; hence, in general, to rise, (figuratively) be majestic.
H1342.kjv: gloriously, grow up, increase, be risen, triumph

H1345.def: from H1342 (גָּאָה) and H410 (אֵל); majesty of God; Geuel, an Israelite.

H1346.def: from H1342 (גָּאָה); arrogance or majesty; by implication, (concretely) ornament.
H1346.kjv: excellency, haughtiness, highness, pride, proudly, swelling

H1347.def: from H1342 (גָּאָה); the same as H1346 (גַּאֲוָה)xlit gaʻăvâh corrected to gaʼăvâh; arrogance or majesty; by implication, (concretely) ornament.
H1347.kjv: arrogancy, excellency(-lent), majesty, pomp, pride, proud, swelling

H1348.def: from H1342 (גָּאָה); the same as H1346 (גַּאֲוָה)xlit gaʻăvâh corrected to gaʼăvâh; arrogance or majesty; by implication, (concretely) ornament.
H1348.kjv: excellent things, lifting up, majesty, pride, proudly, raging

H1350.gloss: to be the next of kin (and as such to buy back a relative
H1350.def: a primitive root, to redeem (according to the Oriental law of kinship), i.e.; to be the next of kin (and as such to buy back a relative's property, marry his widow, etc.).
H1350.kjv: in any wise, at all, avenger, deliver, (do, perform the part of near, next) kinsfolk(-man), purchase, ransom, redeem(-er), revenger

H1351.def: a primitive root, (rather identified with H1350 (גָּאַל), through the idea of freeing, i.e. repudiating); to soil or (figuratively) desecrate.

H1353.gloss: redemption (including the right and the object)
H1353.def: feminine passive participle of H1350 (גָּאַל); redemption (including the right and the object); by implication, relationship.

H1354.def: from an unused root meaning to hollow or curve; (compare H1460 (גֵּו) and H1479 (גּוּף)); the back (as rounded); by analogy, the top or rim, a boss, a vault, arch of eye, bulwarks, etc..
H1354.kjv: back, body, boss, eminent (higher) place, (eye) brows, nave, ring

H1355.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1354 (גַב); the back (as rounded); by analogy, the top or rim, a boss, a vault, arch of eye, bulwarks, etc..

H1356.def: from H1461 (גּוּב); a log (as cut out); also well or cistern (as dug).

H1357.def: probably from H1461 (גּוּב) (compare H1462 (גּוֹב)); a locust (from its cutting).

H1358.gloss: a pit (for wild animals) (as cut out)
H1358.def: (Aramaic) from a root corresponding to H1461 (גּוּב); a pit (for wild animals) (as cut out).

H1359.def: or (fully) גּוֹב; from H1461 (גּוּב); pit; Gob, a place in Palestine.

H1360.def: from an unused root meaning probably to collect; a reservoir; by analogy, a marsh.

H1361.def: a primitive root; to soar, i.e. be lofty; figuratively, to be haughty.
H1361.kjv: exalt, be haughty, be (make) high(-er), lift up, mount up, be proud, raise up great height, upward

H1363.kjv: excellency, haughty, height, high, loftiness, pride

H1364.def: or גָּבוֹהַּ; (fully) from H1361 (גָּבַהּ); elevated (or elated), powerful, arrogant.
H1364.kjv: haughty, height, high(-er), lofty, proud, exceeding proudly

H1366.gloss: a cord (as twisted), i.e. (by implication) a boundary
H1366.def: or גְּבֻל; (shortened) from H1379 (גָּבַל); properly, a cord (as twisted), i.e. (by implication) a boundary; by extension the territory inclosed.
H1366.kjv: border, bound, coast, great, landmark, limit, quarter, space

H1367.def: or גְּבֻלָה; (shortened) feminine of H1366 (גְּבוּל); a boundary, region.

H1368.def: or גִּבֹּר; (shortened) intensive from the same as H1397 (גֶּבֶר); powerful; by implication, warrior, tyrant.
H1368.kjv: champion, chief, excel, giant, man, mighty (man, one), strong (man), valiant man

H1369.def: feminine passive participle from the same as H1368 (גִּבּוֹר); force (literally or figuratively); by implication, valor, victory.
H1369.kjv: force, mastery, might, mighty (act, power), power, strength

H1371.def: from an unused root meaning to be high (in the forehead); bald in the forehead.

H1372.def: from the same as H1371 (גִּבֵּחַ); baldness in the forehead; by analogy, a bare spot on the right side of cloth.

H1373.def: from the same as H1354 (גַב); collective; Gabbai, an Israelite.

H1374.def: plural of H1356 (גֵּב); cisterns; Gebim, a place in Palestine.

H1375.def: from an unused root (meaning to be convex); a goblet; by analogy, the calyx of aflower.

H1378.def: from an unused root (probably meaning to freeze); crystal (from its resemblance to ice).

H1379.def: a primitive root; also as a denominative from H1366 (גְּבוּל); properly, to twist as arope;  to bound (as by a line).

H1380.def: from H1379 (גָּבַל) (in the sense of a chain of hills); a mountain; Gebal, a place in Phoenicia.

H1381.def: the same as H1380 (גְּבַל); Gebal, a region in Idumaea.

H1382.gloss: a Gebalite, or inhabitant of Gebal
H1382.def: ; patrial from H1380 (גְּבַל); a Gebalite, or inhabitant of Gebal.

H1383.def: from H1379 (גָּבַל); a twisted chain or lace.

H1384.def: from an unused root meaning to be arched or contracted; hunch-backed.

H1385.def: feminine from the same as H1384 (גִּבֵּן); curdled milk.

H1386.gloss: a hump or peak of hills
H1386.def: from the same as H1384 (גִּבֵּן); a hump or peak of hills.

H1387.def: from the same as H1375 (גְּבִיעַ), a hillock; Geba, a place in Palestine.

H1388.def: by permutation for H1389 (גִּבְעָה); a hill; Giba, a place in Palestine.

H1389.def: feminine from the same as H1387 (גֶּבַע); a hillock.

H1390.def: the same as H1389 (גִּבְעָה); Gibah; the name of three places in Palestine.

H1391.def: from the same as H1387 (גֶּבַע); hilly; Gibon, a place in Palestine.

H1392.def: prolonged from H1375 (גְּבִיעַ); the calyx of a flower.

H1393.gloss: a Gibonite, or inhabitant of Gibon
H1393.def: patrial from H1391 (גִּבְעוֹן); a Gibonite, or inhabitant of Gibon.

H1394.def: from the same as H1375 (גְּבִיעַ); hilliness; Gibath.

H1395.gloss: a Gibathite, or inhabitant of Gibath
H1395.def: patrial from H1390 (גִּבְעָה); a Gibathite, or inhabitant of Gibath.

H1396.def: a primitive root; to be strong; by implication, to prevail, act insolently.
H1396.kjv: exceed, confirm, be great, be mighty, prevail, put to more (strength), strengthen, be stronger, be valiant

H1397.def: from H1396 (גָּבַר); properly, a valiant man or warrior; generally, a person simply.

H1398.gloss: Geber, the name of two Israelites
H1398.def: the same as H1397 (גֶּבֶר); Geber, the name of two Israelites.

H1399.def: from H1396 (גָּבַר); the same as H1397 (גֶּבֶר); a person.

H1401.def: (Aramaic) intensive of H1400 (גְּבַר); valiant, or warrior.

H1402.def: intensive of H1399 (גְּבַר); Gibbar, an Israelite.

H1403.def: from H1397 (גֶּבֶר) and H410 (אֵל); man of God; Gabriel, an archangel.

H1405.def: intensive from H1389 (גִּבְעָה); a hilly spot; Gibbethon, a place in Palestine.

H1406.def: probably by reduplication from H1342 (גָּאָה); a roof; by analogy, the top of an altar.
H1406.kjv: roof (of the house), (house) top (of the house)

H1407.def: from H1413 (גָּדַד) (in the sense of cutting); coriander seed (from its furrows).

H1408.def: a variation of H1409 (גָּד); Fortune, a Babylonian deity.

H1409.def: from H1464 (גּוּד) (in the sense of distributing); fortune.

H1410.gloss: Gad, a son of Jacob, including his tribe and its territory
H1410.def: from H1464 (גּוּד); Gad, a son of Jacob, including his tribe and its territory; also a prophet.

H1412.gloss: Gudgodah, a place in the Desert
H1412.def: by reduplication from H1413 (גָּדַד) (in the sense of cutting) cleft; Gudgodah, a place in the Desert.

H1413.def: a primitive root (compare H1464 (גּוּד)); to crowd; also to gash (as if by pressing into).
H1413.kjv: assemble (selves by troops), gather (selves together, self in troops), cut selves

H1414.def: (Aramaic) corresponding to H1413 (גָּדַד); to cut down.

H1415.gloss: a border of a river (as cut into by the stream)
H1415.def: from an unused root (meaning to cut off); a border of a river (as cut into by the stream).

H1416.def: from H1413 (גָּדַד); a crowd (especially of soldiers).
H1416.kjv: army, band (of men), company, troop (of robbers)
